{"timestamp":"2026-03-19T04:53:14.166Z","type":"session_meta","payload":{"id":"019d0470-9904-7412-9175-57caf6e1277d","timestamp":"2026-03-19T04:53:01.324Z","cwd":"D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow","originator":"codex-tui","cli_version":"0.115.0","source":"cli","model_provider":"crs","base_instructions":{"text":"You are Codex, a coding agent based on GPT-5. You and the user share the same workspace and collaborate to achieve the user's goals.\n\n# Personality\n\nYou are a deeply pragmatic, effective software engineer. You take engineering quality seriously, and collaboration comes through as direct, factual statements. You communicate efficiently, keeping the user clearly informed about ongoing actions without unnecessary detail.\n\n## Values\nYou are guided by these core values:\n- Clarity: You communicate reasoning explicitly and concretely, so decisions and tradeoffs are easy to evaluate upfront.\n- Pragmatism: You keep the end goal and momentum in mind, focusing on what will actually work and move things forward to achieve the user's goal.\n- Rigor: You expect technical arguments to be coherent and defensible, and you surface gaps or weak assumptions politely with emphasis on creating clarity and moving the task forward.\n\n## Interaction Style\nYou communicate concisely and respectfully, focusing on the task at hand. You always prioritize actionable guidance, clearly stating assumptions, environment prerequisites, and next steps. Unless explicitly asked, you avoid excessively verbose explanations about your work.\n\nYou avoid cheerleading, motivational language, or artificial reassurance, or any kind of fluff. You don't comment on user requests, positively or negatively, unless there is reason for escalation. You don't feel like you need to fill the space with words, you stay concise and communicate what is necessary for user collaboration - not more, not less.\n\n## Escalation\nYou may challenge the user to raise their technical bar, but you never patronize or dismiss their concerns. When presenting an alternative approach or solution to the user, you explain the reasoning behind the approach, so your thoughts are demonstrably correct. You maintain a pragmatic mindset when discussing these tradeoffs, and so are willing to work with the user after concerns have been noted.\n\n\n# General\nAs an expert coding agent, your primary focus is writing code, answering questions, and helping the user complete their task in the current environment. You build context by examining the codebase first without making assumptions or jumping to conclusions. You think through the nuances of the code you encounter, and embody the mentality of a skilled senior software engineer.\n\n- When searching for text or files, prefer using `rg` or `rg --files` respectively because `rg` is much faster than alternatives like `grep`. (If the `rg` command is not found, then use alternatives.)\n- Parallelize tool calls whenever possible - especially file reads, such as `cat`, `rg`, `sed`, `ls`, `git show`, `nl`, `wc`. Use `multi_tool_use.parallel` to parallelize tool calls and only this. Never chain together bash commands with separators like `echo \"====\";` as this renders to the user poorly.\n\n## Editing constraints\n\n- Default to ASCII when editing or creating files. Only introduce non-ASCII or other Unicode characters when there is a clear justification and the file already uses them.\n- Add succinct code comments that explain what is going on if code is not self-explanatory. You should not add comments like \"Assigns the value to the variable\", but a brief comment might be useful ahead of a complex code block that the user would otherwise have to spend time parsing out. Usage of these comments should be rare.\n- Always use apply_patch for manual code edits. Do not use cat or any other commands when creating or editing files. Formatting commands or bulk edits don't need to be done with apply_patch.\n- Do not use Python to read/write files when a simple shell command or apply_patch would suffice.\n- You may be in a dirty git worktree.\n  * NEVER revert existing changes you did not make unless explicitly requested, since these changes were made by the user.\n  * If asked to make a commit or code edits and there are unrelated changes to your work or changes that you didn't make in those files, don't revert those changes.\n  * If the changes are in files you've touched recently, you should read carefully and understand how you can work with the changes rather than reverting them.\n  * If the changes are in unrelated files, just ignore them and don't revert them.\n- Do not amend a commit unless explicitly requested to do so.\n- While you are working, you might notice unexpected changes that you didn't make. It's likely the user made them, or were autogenerated. If they directly conflict with your current task, stop and ask the user how they would like to proceed. Otherwise, focus on the task at hand.\n- **NEVER** use destructive commands like `git reset --hard` or `git checkout --` unless specifically requested or approved by the user.\n- You struggle using the git interactive console. **ALWAYS** prefer using non-interactive git commands.\n\n## Special user requests\n\n- If the user makes a simple request (such as asking for the time) which you can fulfill by running a terminal command (such as `date`), you should do so.\n- If the user asks for a \"review\", default to a code review mindset: prioritise identifying bugs, risks, behavioural regressions, and missing tests. Findings must be the primary focus of the response - keep summaries or overviews brief and only after enumerating the issues. Present findings first (ordered by severity with file/line references), follow with open questions or assumptions, and offer a change-summary only as a secondary detail. If no findings are discovered, state that explicitly and mention any residual risks or testing gaps.\n\n## Autonomy and persistence\nPersist until the task is fully handled end-to-end within the current turn whenever feasible: do not stop at analysis or partial fixes; carry changes through implementation, verification, and a clear explanation of outcomes unless the user explicitly pauses or redirects you.\n\nUnless the user explicitly asks for a plan, asks a question about the code, is brainstorming potential solutions, or some other intent that makes it clear that code should not be written, assume the user wants you to make code changes or run tools to solve the user's problem. In these cases, it's bad to output your proposed solution in a message, you should go ahead and actually implement the change. If you encounter challenges or blockers, you should attempt to resolve them yourself.\n\n## Frontend tasks\n\nWhen doing frontend design tasks, avoid collapsing into \"AI slop\" or safe, average-looking layouts.\nAim for interfaces that feel intentional, bold, and a bit surprising.\n- Typography: Use expressive, purposeful fonts and avoid default stacks (Inter, Roboto, Arial, system).\n- Color & Look: Choose a clear visual direction; define CSS variables; avoid purple-on-white defaults. No purple bias or dark mode bias.\n- Motion: Use a few meaningful animations (page-load, staggered reveals) instead of generic micro-motions.\n- Background: Don't rely on flat, single-color backgrounds; use gradients, shapes, or subtle patterns to build atmosphere.\n- Ensure the page loads properly on both desktop and mobile\n- For React code, prefer modern patterns including useEffectEvent, startTransition, and useDeferredValue when appropriate if used by the team. Do not add useMemo/useCallback by default unless already used; follow the repo's React Compiler guidance.\n- Overall: Avoid boilerplate layouts and interchangeable UI patterns. Vary themes, type families, and visual languages across outputs.\n\nException: If working within an existing website or design system, preserve the established patterns, structure, and visual language.\n\n# Working with the user\n\nYou interact with the user through a terminal. You have 2 ways of communicating with the users:\n- Share intermediary updates in `commentary` channel. \n- After you have completed all your work, send a message to the `final` channel.\nYou are producing plain text that will later be styled by the program you run in. Formatting should make results easy to scan, but not feel mechanical. Use judgment to decide how much structure adds value. Follow the formatting rules exactly.\n\n## Formatting rules\n\n- You may format with GitHub-flavored Markdown.\n- Structure your answer if necessary, the complexity of the answer should match the task. If the task is simple, your answer should be a one-liner. Order sections from general to specific to supporting.\n- Never use nested bullets. Keep lists flat (single level). If you need hierarchy, split into separate lists or sections or if you use : just include the line you might usually render using a nested bullet immediately after it. For numbered lists, only use the `1. 2. 3.` style markers (with a period), never `1)`.\n- Headers are optional, only use them when you think they are necessary. If you do use them, use short Title Case (1-3 words) wrapped in **鈥?*. Don't add a blank line.\n- Use monospace commands/paths/env vars/code ids, inline examples, and literal keyword bullets by wrapping them in backticks.\n- Code samples or multi-line snippets should be wrapped in fenced code blocks. Include an info string as often as possible.\n- File References: When referencing files in your response follow the below rules:\n  * Use markdown links (not inline code) for clickable file paths.\n  * Each reference should have a stand alone path. Even if it's the same file.\n  * For clickable/openable file references, the path target must be an absolute filesystem path. Labels may be short (for example, `[app.ts](/abs/path/app.ts)`).\n  * Optionally include line/column (1鈥慴ased): :line[:column] or #Lline[Ccolumn] (column defaults to 1).\n  * Do not use URIs like file://, vscode://, or https://.\n  * Do not provide range of lines\n- Don鈥檛 use emojis or em dashes unless explicitly instructed.\n\n## Final answer instructions\n\nAlways favor conciseness in your final answer - you should usually avoid long-winded explanations and focus only on the most important details. For casual chit-chat, just chat. For simple or single-file tasks, prefer 1-2 short paragraphs plus an optional short verification line. Do not default to bullets. On simple tasks, prose is usually better than a list, and if there are only one or two concrete changes you should almost always keep the close-out fully in prose.\n\nOn larger tasks, use at most 2-4 high-level sections when helpful. Each section can be a short paragraph or a few flat bullets. Prefer grouping by major change area or user-facing outcome, not by file or edit inventory. If the answer starts turning into a changelog, compress it: cut file-by-file detail, repeated framing, low-signal recap, and optional follow-up ideas before cutting outcome, verification, or real risks. Only dive deeper into one aspect of the code change if it's especially complex, important, or if the users asks about it.\n\nRequirements for your final answer:\n- Prefer short paragraphs by default.\n- Use lists only when the content is inherently list-shaped: enumerating distinct items, steps, options, categories, comparisons, ideas. Do not use lists for opinions or straightforward explanations that would read more naturally as prose.\n- Do not turn simple explanations into outlines or taxonomies unless the user asks for depth. If a list is used, each bullet should be a complete standalone point.\n- Do not begin responses with conversational interjections or meta commentary. Avoid openers such as acknowledgements (鈥淒one 鈥斺€? 鈥淕ot it鈥? 鈥淕reat question, 鈥? \"You're right to call that out\") or framing phrases.\n- The user does not see command execution outputs. When asked to show the output of a command (e.g. `git show`), relay the important details in your answer or summarize the key lines so the user understands the result.\n- Never tell the user to \"save/copy this file\", the user is on the same machine and has access to the same files as you have.\n- If the user asks for a code explanation, include code references as appropriate.\n- If you weren't able to do something, for example run tests, tell the user.\n- Never use nested bullets. Keep lists flat (single level). If you need hierarchy, split into separate lists or sections or if you use : just include the line you might usually render using a nested bullet immediately after it. For numbered lists, only use the `1. 2. 3.` style markers (with a period), never `1)`.\n\n## Intermediary updates \n\n- Intermediary updates go to the `commentary` channel.\n- User updates are short updates while you are working, they are NOT final answers.\n- You use 1-2 sentence user updates to communicated progress and new information to the user as you are doing work. \n- Do not begin responses with conversational interjections or meta commentary. Avoid openers such as acknowledgements (鈥淒one 鈥斺€? 鈥淕ot it鈥? 鈥淕reat question, 鈥? or framing phrases.\n- Before exploring or doing substantial work, you start with a user update acknowledging the request and explaining your first step. You should include your understanding of the user request and explain what you will do. Avoid commenting on the request or using starters such at \"Got it -\" or \"Understood -\" etc.\n- You provide user updates frequently, every 30s.\n- When exploring, e.g. searching, reading files you provide user updates as you go, explaining what context you are gathering and what you've learned. Vary your sentence structure when providing these updates to avoid sounding repetitive - in particular, don't start each sentence the same way.\n- When working for a while, keep updates informative and varied, but stay concise.\n- After you have sufficient context, and the work is substantial you provide a longer plan (this is the only user update that may be longer than 2 sentences and can contain formatting).\n- Before performing file edits of any kind, you provide updates explaining what edits you are making.\n- As you are thinking, you very frequently provide updates even if not taking any actions, informing the user of your progress. You interrupt your thinking and send multiple updates in a row if thinking for more than 100 words.\n- Tone of your updates MUST match your personality.\n"},"git":{"commit_hash":"03dc55d30fe458d497142ae3ffca20c8c36e8c7b","branch":"main","repository_url":"https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow.git"}}}

Network access is enabled.\nApproval policy is currently never. Do not provide the `sandbox_permissions` for any reason, commands will be rejected.\r\n</permissions instructions>"},{"type":"input_text","text":"<collaboration_mode># Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n</collaboration_mode>"},{"type":"input_text","text":"<skills_instructions>\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- memorytree-workflow: Detect, initialize, and maintain a reusable MemoryTree project-memory workspace for any code repository. On activation, immediately detect and report the repo's MemoryTree state. (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/SKILL.md)\n- openai-docs: Use when the user asks how to build with OpenAI products or APIs and needs up-to-date official documentation with citations, help choosing the latest model for a use case, or explicit GPT-5.4 upgrade and prompt-upgrade guidance; prioritize OpenAI docs MCP tools, use bundled references only as helper context, and restrict any fallback browsing to official OpenAI domains. (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/openai-docs/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.\n</skills_instructions>"}]}}

dist\chunk-BUGFFTLQ.js.map:1:{"version":3,"sources":["../src/transcript/discover.ts","../src/transcript/import.ts","../src/transcript/db.ts"],"sourcesContent":["/**\r\n * Transcript discovery — file scanning and project matching.\r\n * Port of scripts/_transcript_discover.py\r\n */\r\n\r\nimport { existsSync, statSync } from 'node:fs'\r\nimport { homedir } from 'node:os'\r\nimport { basename, dirname, resolve } from 'node:path'\r\n\r\nimport fg from 'fast-glob'\r\n\r\nimport type { ParsedTranscript } from '../types/transcript.js'\r\nimport { slugify, CLIENTS } from './common.js'\r\nimport { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Glob patterns per client\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nconst CLIENT_PATTERNS: Readonly<Record<string, readonly string[]>> = {\r\n  codex: ['sessions/**/*.jsonl'],\r\n  claude: ['projects/**/*.jsonl'],\r\n  gemini: [\r\n    'tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.json',\r\n    'tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.jsonl',\r\n    'history/**/*.json',\r\n    'history/**/*.jsonl',\r\n    'chats/**/*.json',\r\n    'chats/**/*.jsonl',\r\n  ],\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Defaults\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot(): string {\r\n  return toPosixPath(resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'transcripts'))\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function defaultClientRoots(): Record<string, string> {\r\n  const home = homedir()\r\n  return {\r\n    codex: resolve(home, '.codex'),\r\n    claude: resolve(home, '.claude'),\r\n    gemini: resolve(home, '.gemini'),\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// File discovery\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function discoverSourceFiles(\r\n  clients?: ReadonlySet<string>,\r\n  roots?: Readonly<Record<string, string>>,\r\n): Array<[string, string]> {\r\n  const requested = clients ?? CLIENTS\r\n  const resolvedRoots = roots ?? defaultClientRoots()\r\n  const matches: Array<[string, string]> = []\r\n  const seen = new Set<string>()\r\n\r\n  for (const client of [...requested].sort()) {\r\n    const root: string | undefined = resolvedRoots[client]\r\n    if (root === undefined || !existsSync(root)) continue\r\n\r\n    const patterns = CLIENT_PATTERNS[client]\r\n    if (patterns === undefined) continue\r\n\r\n    for (const pattern of patterns) {\r\n      const paths = fg.sync(pattern, { cwd: root, absolute: true, onlyFiles: true })\r\n      for (const filePath of paths) {\r\n        const resolved = toPosixPath(resolve(filePath))\r\n        const key = resolved.toLowerCase()\r\n        if (seen.has(key)) continue\r\n        seen.add(key)\r\n        matches.push([client, resolved])\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  matches.sort((a, b) => {\r\n    const mtimeA = safeFileMtime(a[1])\r\n    const mtimeB = safeFileMtime(b[1])\r\n    if (mtimeB !== mtimeA) return mtimeB - mtimeA\r\n    const pathA = a[1].toLowerCase()\r\n    const pathB = b[1].toLowerCase()\r\n    // Reverse sort: higher path string comes first (matching Python's reverse=True)\r\n    if (pathB > pathA) return 1\r\n    if (pathB < pathA) return -1\r\n    return 0\r\n  })\r\n\r\n  return matches\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Project slug inference\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function inferProjectSlug(parsed: ParsedTranscript): string {\r\n  if (parsed.cwd) {\r\n    try {\r\n      const cwdBase = basename(parsed.cwd)\r\n      return slugify(cwdBase, 'project')\r\n    } catch {\r\n      // fall through\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  if (parsed.client === 'claude') {\r\n    let parentName = basename(dirname(parsed.source_path)).toLowerCase()\r\n    parentName = parentName.replace(/^[a-z]--/, '')\r\n    return slugify(parentName, 'project')\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  if (parsed.client === 'gemini') {\r\n    const parentDir = basename(dirname(parsed.source_path))\r\n    if (parentDir) {\r\n      return slugify(parentDir, 'project')\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  return 'unknown-project'\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Repo matching\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function transcriptMatchesRepo(\r\n  parsed: ParsedTranscript,\r\n  repoRoot: string,\r\n  repoSlug: string,\r\n): boolean {\r\n  const normalizedRepo = toPosixPath(resolve(repoRoot)).toLowerCase()\r\n\r\n  if (parsed.cwd) {\r\n    try {\r\n      const normalizedCwd = toPosixPath(resolve(parsed.cwd)).toLowerCase()\r\n      if (normalizedCwd === normalizedRepo) return true\r\n      // Check if repoRoot is a parent of cwd\r\n      const repoPrefix = normalizedRepo.endsWith('/')\r\n        ? normalizedRepo\r\n        : normalizedRepo + '/'\r\n      if (normalizedCwd.startsWith(repoPrefix)) return true\r\n    } catch {\r\n      // fall through\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  return projectSlugsMatch(inferProjectSlug(parsed), repoSlug)\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Slug matching\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function projectSlugsMatch(left: string, right: string): boolean {\r\n  return Boolean(\r\n    left && right && left === right && left !== 'unknown-project',\r\n  )\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Safe mtime\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function safeFileMtime(filePath: string): number {\r\n  try {\r\n    return statSync(filePath).mtimeMs / 1000\r\n  } catch {\r\n    return 0\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n","/**\r\n * Transcript import — orchestration, cleaning, and manifest management.\r\n * Port of scripts/_transcript_import.py\r\n */\r\n\r\nimport { copyFileSync, existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, appendFileSync } from 'node:fs'\r\nimport { dirname, extname, join, basename, relative } from 'node:path'\r\n\r\nimport type { ManifestEntry, ParsedTranscript, TranscriptArtifact } from '../types/transcript.js'\r\nimport { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'\r\nimport {\r\n  loadJson,\r\n  normalizeTimestamp,\r\n  sha256File,\r\n  slugify,\r\n  timestampPartition,\r\n  yamlEscape,\r\n} from './common.js'\r\nimport { upsertSearchIndex } from './db.js'\r\n\r\nexport async function importTranscript(\r\n  parsed: ParsedTranscript,\r\n  root: string,\r\n  globalRoot: string,\r\n  projectSlug: string,\r\n  rawUploadPermission: string,\r\n  mirrorToRepo = true,\r\n): Promise<ManifestEntry> {\r\n  const importedAt = normalizeTimestamp(new Date())\r\n  const rawSha256 = sha256File(parsed.source_path)\r\n  const sessionLabel = slugify(\r\n    parsed.session_id,\r\n    slugify(stemOf(parsed.source_path), 'session'),\r\n  )\r\n  const artifactStem = `${sessionLabel}__${rawSha256.slice(0, 8)}`\r\n  const sourceSuffix = extname(parsed.source_path) || '.txt'\r\n\r\n  const [yearToken, monthToken] = timestampPartition(parsed.started_at || importedAt)\r\n\r\n  const repoRoot = join(root, 'Memory', '06_transcripts')\r\n  const repoRawPath = join(repoRoot, 'raw', parsed.client, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}${sourceSuffix}`)\r\n  const repoCleanPath = join(repoRoot, 'clean', parsed.client, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}.md`)\r\n  const repoManifestPath = join(repoRoot, 'manifests', parsed.client, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}.json`)\r\n  const repoFullPath = join(repoRoot, 'full', parsed.client, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}.json`)\r\n\r\n  const globalRawPath = join(globalRoot, 'raw', parsed.client, projectSlug, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}${sourceSuffix}`)\r\n  const globalCleanPath = join(globalRoot, 'clean', parsed.client, projectSlug, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}.md`)\r\n  const globalManifestPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'manifests', parsed.client, projectSlug, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}.json`)\r\n  const globalFullPath = join(globalRoot, 'full', parsed.client, projectSlug, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}.json`)\r\n  const globalEventLogPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'sessions.jsonl')\r\n  const globalDbPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'search.sqlite')\r\n\r\n  for (const p of [globalRawPath, globalCleanPath, globalManifestPath, globalFullPath, globalEventLogPath, globalDbPath]) {\r\n    mkdirSync(dirname(p), { recursive: true })\r\n  }\r\n  if (mirrorToRepo) {\r\n    for (const p of [repoRawPath, repoCleanPath, repoManifestPath, repoFullPath]) {\r\n      mkdirSync(dirname(p), { recursive: true })\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  if (mirrorToRepo) {\r\n    copyFile(parsed.source_path, repoRawPath)\r\n  }\r\n  copyFile(parsed.source_path, globalRawPath)\r\n\r\n  let manifest: ManifestEntry = {\r\n    client: parsed.client,\r\n    project: projectSlug,\r\n    session_id: parsed.session_id,\r\n    title: parsed.title,\r\n    started_at: parsed.started_at,\r\n    imported_at: importedAt,\r\n    cwd: parsed.cwd,\r\n    branch: parsed.branch,\r\n    raw_source_path: toPosixPath(parsed.source_path),\r\n    raw_sha256: rawSha256,\r\n    raw_upload_permission: rawUploadPermission,\r\n    repo_raw_path: mirrorToRepo ? toPosixPath(relative(root, repoRawPath)) : '',\r\n    repo_clean_path: mirrorToRepo ? toPosixPath(relative(root, repoCleanPath)) : '',\r\n    repo_manifest_path: mirrorToRepo ? toPosixPath(relative(root, repoManifestPath)) : '',\r\n    repo_full_path: mirrorToRepo ? toPosixPath(relative(root, repoFullPath)) : '',\r\n    global_raw_path: toPosixPath(globalRawPath),\r\n    global_clean_path: toPosixPath(globalCleanPath),\r\n    global_manifest_path: toPosixPath(globalManifestPath),\r\n    global_full_path: toPosixPath(globalFullPath),\r\n    message_count: parsed.messages.length,\r\n    tool_event_count: parsed.tool_events.length,\r\n    event_count: parsed.events.length,\r\n    cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',\r\n    repo_mirror_enabled: mirrorToRepo,\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const existingGlobalManifest = loadJson(globalManifestPath)\r\n  manifest = preserveExistingImportTimestamp(existingGlobalManifest, manifest)\r\n\r\n  if (mirrorToRepo) {\r\n    writeCleanMarkdown(parsed, manifest, repoCleanPath)\r\n    writeFullJson(parsed, repoFullPath)\r\n  }\r\n  writeCleanMarkdown(parsed, manifest, globalCleanPath)\r\n  writeFullJson(parsed, globalFullPath)\r\n  const manifestRecord = { ...manifest } as Record<string, unknown>\r\n  if (mirrorToRepo) {\r\n    writeJson(repoManifestPath, manifestRecord)\r\n  }\r\n  const appendToEventLog = existingGlobalManifest === null || !deepEqual(existingGlobalManifest, manifestRecord)\r\n  writeJson(globalManifestPath, manifestRecord)\r\n  if (appendToEventLog) {\r\n    appendJsonl(globalEventLogPath, manifestRecord)\r\n  }\r\n  await upsertSearchIndex(globalDbPath, manifest)\r\n\r\n  return manifest\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function transcriptHasContent(parsed: ParsedTranscript): boolean {\r\n  return parsed.messages.length > 0 || parsed.tool_events.length > 0 || parsed.events.length > 0\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function writeCleanMarkdown(parsed: ParsedTranscript, manifest: ManifestEntry, filePath: string): void {\r\n  const lines: string[] = [\r\n    '---',\r\n    `client: ${manifest.client}`,\r\n    `project: ${manifest.project}`,\r\n    `session_id: ${manifest.session_id}`,\r\n    `title: ${yamlEscape(manifest.title)}`,\r\n    `started_at: ${manifest.started_at}`,\r\n    `imported_at: ${manifest.imported_at}`,\r\n    `cwd: ${yamlEscape(manifest.cwd)}`,\r\n    `branch: ${yamlEscape(manifest.branch)}`,\r\n    `raw_source_path: ${yamlEscape(manifest.raw_source_path)}`,\r\n    `raw_sha256: ${manifest.raw_sha256}`,\r\n    `raw_upload_permission: ${manifest.raw_upload_permission}`,\r\n    `cleaning_mode: ${manifest.cleaning_mode}`,\r\n    '---',\r\n    '',\r\n    `# ${manifest.title || manifest.session_id}`,\r\n    '',\r\n    '## Metadata',\r\n    `- Client: \\`${manifest.client}\\``,\r\n    `- Project: \\`${manifest.project}\\``,\r\n    `- Session ID: \\`${manifest.session_id}\\``,\r\n    `- Started At: \\`${manifest.started_at}\\``,\r\n    `- Imported At: \\`${manifest.imported_at}\\``,\r\n    `- Raw SHA256: \\`${manifest.raw_sha256}\\``,\r\n    `- Raw Source: \\`${manifest.raw_source_path}\\``,\r\n    `- Repo Raw Path: \\`${manifest.repo_raw_path}\\``,\r\n    `- Repo Clean Path: \\`${manifest.repo_clean_path}\\``,\r\n    '',\r\n    '## Messages',\r\n  ]\r\n\r\n  if (parsed.messages.length > 0) {\r\n    for (let i = 0; i < parsed.messages.length; i++) {\r\n      const message = parsed.messages[i]!\r\n      lines.push(\r\n        `### ${i + 1}. ${message.role}`,\r\n        `- Timestamp: \\`${message.timestamp ?? manifest.started_at}\\``,\r\n        '',\r\n        message.text,\r\n        '',\r\n      )\r\n    }\r\n  } else {\r\n    lines.push(\r\n      'No user or assistant messages were extracted deterministically from the source transcript.',\r\n      '',\r\n    )\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  writeFileSync(filePath, lines.join('\\n'), 'utf-8')\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function writeFullJson(parsed: ParsedTranscript, filePath: string): void {\r\n  const artifact: TranscriptArtifact = {\r\n    schema_version: 'transcript.full.v1',\r\n    ...parsed,\r\n  }\r\n  writeFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify(artifact, null, 2) + '\\n', 'utf-8')\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function preserveExistingImportTimestamp(\r\n  existing: Record<string, unknown> | null,\r\n  payload: ManifestEntry,\r\n): ManifestEntry {\r\n  if (existing === null) return payload\r\n  const payloadRecord = { ...payload } as Record<string, unknown>\r\n  if (!deepEqual(manifestSignature(existing), manifestSignature(payloadRecord))) return payload\r\n  const importedAt = existing['imported_at']\r\n  if (typeof importedAt === 'string' && importedAt) {\r\n    return { ...payload, imported_at: importedAt }\r\n  }\r\n  return payload\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function manifestSignature(payload: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown> {\r\n  const result: Record<string, unknown> = {}\r\n  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(payload)) {\r\n    if (key !== 'imported_at') {\r\n      result[key] = value\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n  return result\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function manifestChanged(filePath: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>): boolean {\r\n  if (!existsSync(filePath)) return true\r\n  try {\r\n    const current = JSON.parse(readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')) as Record<string, unknown>\r\n    return !deepEqual(current, payload)\r\n  } catch {\r\n    return true\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function writeJson(filePath: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>): void {\r\n  writeFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2) + '\\n', 'utf-8')\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function appendJsonl(filePath: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>): void {\r\n  appendFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify(payload) + '\\n', 'utf-8')\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function copyFile(source: string, destination: string): void {\r\n  if (source === destination) return\r\n  copyFileSync(source, destination)\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Internal helpers\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nfunction stemOf(filePath: string): string {\r\n  const base = basename(filePath)\r\n  const ext = extname(base)\r\n  return ext ? base.slice(0, -ext.length) : base\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction deepEqual(a: unknown, b: unknown): boolean {\r\n  if (a === b) return true\r\n  if (a === null || b === null || typeof a !== typeof b) return false\r\n  if (typeof a !== 'object') return false\r\n  if (Array.isArray(a) !== Array.isArray(b)) return false\r\n  const aObj = a as Record<string, unknown>\r\n  const bObj = b as Record<string, unknown>\r\n  const aKeys = Object.keys(aObj)\r\n  const bKeys = Object.keys(bObj)\r\n  if (aKeys.length !== bKeys.length) return false\r\n  return aKeys.every(key => deepEqual(aObj[key], bObj[key]))\r\n}\r\n","/**\r\n * Transcript search index — SQLite upsert for the global transcript catalog.\r\n *\r\n * Uses sql.js (WebAssembly SQLite) so the module works without a native\r\n * sqlite3 binding.  The WASM init is async; we cache the resulting factory\r\n * promise so it is only resolved once per process.\r\n */\r\n\r\nimport initSqlJs, { type SqlJsStatic } from 'sql.js'\r\nimport { existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'\r\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '../types/transcript.js'\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Lazy sql.js initialisation (cached promise)\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nlet sqlPromise: Promise<SqlJsStatic> | null = null\r\n\r\nfunction getSqlJs(): Promise<SqlJsStatic> {\r\n  if (sqlPromise === null) {\r\n    sqlPromise = initSqlJs()\r\n  }\r\n  return sqlPromise\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Column definitions\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nconst PK_COLUMNS = [\r\n  'client',\r\n  'project',\r\n  'session_id',\r\n  'raw_sha256',\r\n] as const\r\n\r\nconst DATA_COLUMNS = [\r\n  'title',\r\n  'started_at',\r\n  'imported_at',\r\n  'cwd',\r\n  'branch',\r\n  'raw_source_path',\r\n  'raw_upload_permission',\r\n  'global_raw_path',\r\n  'global_clean_path',\r\n  'global_manifest_path',\r\n  'global_full_path',\r\n  'repo_raw_path',\r\n  'repo_clean_path',\r\n  'repo_manifest_path',\r\n  'repo_full_path',\r\n  'message_count',\r\n  'tool_event_count',\r\n  'event_count',\r\n] as const\r\n\r\nconst ALL_COLUMNS = [...PK_COLUMNS, ...DATA_COLUMNS] as const\r\ntype ColumnName = (typeof ALL_COLUMNS)[number]\r\n\r\nconst COLUMN_DEFS: Record<ColumnName, { type: 'TEXT' | 'INTEGER'; defaultValue: string }> = {\r\n  client: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  project: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  session_id: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  raw_sha256: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  title: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  started_at: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  imported_at: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  cwd: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  branch: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  raw_source_path: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  raw_upload_permission: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  global_raw_path: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  global_clean_path: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  global_manifest_path: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  global_full_path: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  repo_raw_path: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  repo_clean_path: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  repo_manifest_path: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  repo_full_path: { type: 'TEXT', defaultValue: \"''\" },\r\n  message_count: { type: 'INTEGER', defaultValue: '0' },\r\n  tool_event_count: { type: 'INTEGER', defaultValue: '0' },\r\n  event_count: { type: 'INTEGER', defaultValue: '0' },\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// SQL statements (built once)\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nconst CREATE_TABLE_SQL = `\r\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (\r\n    ${ALL_COLUMNS.map((column) => columnDefinition(column)).join(',\\n    ')},\r\n    PRIMARY KEY (client, pro…104753 tokens truncated….stdout.write(`Project registered: ${yesNo(status.fragmentExists)}\\n`)\n  process.stdout.write(`Port: ${String(status.port)}\\n`)\n  process.stdout.write(`Exposure: ${status.exposure}\\n`)\n  process.stdout.write(`Report directory: ${status.reportDir}\\n`)\n  process.stdout.write(`Main config: ${status.mainConfigPath}\\n`)\n  process.stdout.write(`Project fragment: ${status.fragmentPath}\\n`)\n  for (const url of status.localUrls) {\n    process.stdout.write(`Local URL: ${url}\\n`)\n  }\n  for (const url of status.lanUrls) {\n    process.stdout.write(`LAN URL: ${url}\\n`)\n  }\n  if (!status.installed || !status.running || status.usingManagedConfig !== true) {\n    process.stdout.write(`Managed start command: ${managedCaddyStartCommand()}\\n`)\n  }\n  return 0\n}\n\nfunction resolveProject(root: string): ProjectEntry | null {\n  const config = loadConfig()\n  return findProjectForPath(config, resolve(root))\n}\n\nfunction yesNo(value: boolean): string {\n  return value ? 'yes' : 'no'\n}\n\nfunction yesNoOrUnknown(value: boolean | null): string {\n  if (value === null) return 'unknown'\n  return yesNo(value)\n}\n\nfunction formatError(error: unknown): string {\n  if (error instanceof Error && error.message) {\n    return error.message\n  }\n  return String(error)\n}\n","import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, readdirSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { networkInterfaces } from 'node:os'\nimport { resolve } from 'node:path'\n\nimport { memorytreeRoot, type ProjectEntry } from '../heartbeat/config.js'\nimport { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'\nimport { execCommand } from '../utils/exec.js'\n\nconst CADDY_ADMIN_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:2019'\nconst CADDY_ADMIN_ORIGIN = 'http://127.0.0.1:2019'\n\nexport interface ManagedCaddyPaths {\n  readonly rootDir: string\n  readonly sitesDir: string\n  readonly mainConfigPath: string\n  readonly fragmentPath: string\n}\n\nexport interface ManagedCaddyStatus {\n  readonly installed: boolean\n  readonly running: boolean\n  readonly usingManagedConfig: boolean | null\n  readonly mainConfigPath: string\n  readonly mainConfigExists: boolean\n  readonly fragmentPath: string\n  readonly fragmentExists: boolean\n  readonly port: number\n  readonly exposure: ProjectEntry['report_exposure']\n  readonly reportDir: string\n  readonly localUrls: readonly string[]\n  readonly lanUrls: readonly string[]\n}\n\nexport interface ManagedCaddyEnableResult {\n  readonly mainConfigPath: string\n  readonly fragmentPath: string\n  readonly reportDir: string\n  readonly localUrls: readonly string[]\n  readonly lanUrls: readonly string[]\n}\n\nexport function managedCaddyPaths(projectId: string): ManagedCaddyPaths {\n  const rootDir = resolve(memorytreeRoot(), 'caddy')\n  const sitesDir = resolve(rootDir, 'sites')\n  return {\n    rootDir,\n    sitesDir,\n    mainConfigPath: resolve(rootDir, 'Caddyfile'),\n    fragmentPath: resolve(sitesDir, `${projectId}.caddy`),\n  }\n}\n\nexport function projectReportDir(project: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'development_path'>): string {\n  return resolve(project.development_path, 'Memory', '07_reports')\n}\n\nexport function renderManagedMainCaddyfile(): string {\n  return [\n    '# Managed by MemoryTree. Do not edit by hand.',\n    '{',\n    '  auto_https off',\n    '}',\n    '',\n    'import sites/*.caddy',\n    '',\n  ].join('\\n')\n}\n\nexport function renderManagedProjectFragment(\n  project: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'id' | 'name' | 'report_port' | 'report_exposure'>,\n  reportDir: string,\n): string {\n  const lines = [\n    '# Managed by MemoryTree. Do not edit by hand.',\n    `# project_id: ${project.id}`,\n    `# project_name: ${project.name}`,\n    `# report_port: ${String(project.report_port)}`,\n    `# report_exposure: ${project.report_exposure}`,\n    '',\n    managedSiteAddress(project.report_port, project.report_exposure),\n    '{',\n  ]\n\n  if (project.report_exposure === 'local') {\n    lines.push('  bind 127.0.0.1 [::1]')\n  }\n\n  lines.push(`  root * ${quoteForCaddyPath(reportDir)}`)\n  lines.push('  file_server')\n  lines.push('}')\n  lines.push('')\n  return lines.join('\\n')\n}\n\nexport function isCaddyInstalled(): boolean {\n  try {\n    execCommand('caddy', ['version'])\n    return true\n  } catch {\n    return false\n  }\n}\n\nexport function findManagedPortConflict(projectId: string, reportPort: number): string | null {\n  const { sitesDir, fragmentPath } = managedCaddyPaths(projectId)\n  if (!existsSync(sitesDir)) return null\n\n  for (const entry of readdirSync(sitesDir)) {\n    if (!entry.endsWith('.caddy')) continue\n    const currentPath = resolve(sitesDir, entry)\n    if (currentPath === fragmentPath) continue\n\n    const text = readFileSync(currentPath, 'utf-8')\n    const portMatch = text.match(/^# report_port:\\s*(\\d+)\\s*$/m)\n    if (!portMatch) continue\n\n    const currentPort = parseInt(portMatch[1]!, 10)\n    if (currentPort !== reportPort) continue\n\n    const projectMatch = text.match(/^# project_id:\\s*(.+)\\s*$/m)\n    return projectMatch?.[1]?.trim() || entry.replace(/\\.caddy$/i, '')\n  }\n\n  return null\n}\n\nexport function enableManagedCaddy(project: ProjectEntry): ManagedCaddyEnableResult {\n  const paths = managedCaddyPaths(project.id)\n  const conflictProjectId = findManagedPortConflict(project.id, project.report_port)\n  if (conflictProjectId !== null) {\n    throw new Error(\n      `Port ${String(project.report_port)} is already used by another managed Caddy site (${conflictProjectId}). ` +\n      'Change this project\\'s report_port first, then rerun the command.',\n    )\n  }\n\n  mkdirSync(paths.rootDir, { recursive: true })\n  mkdirSync(paths.sitesDir, { recursive: true })\n\n  const reportDir = projectReportDir(project)\n  mkdirSync(reportDir, { recursive: true })\n\n  writeTextIfChanged(paths.mainConfigPath, renderManagedMainCaddyfile())\n  writeTextIfChanged(paths.fragmentPath, renderManagedProjectFragment(project, reportDir))\n  validateManagedCaddyConfig(paths.mainConfigPath)\n  try {\n    reloadManagedCaddy(paths.mainConfigPath)\n  } catch (error: unknown) {\n    throw new Error(\n      `Caddy config files were updated, but reload failed. ${formatExecError(error)} ` +\n      `Start Caddy with: ${managedCaddyStartCommand()}`,\n    )\n  }\n\n  return {\n    mainConfigPath: paths.mainConfigPath,\n    fragmentPath: paths.fragmentPath,\n    reportDir,\n    localUrls: localUrlsForProject(project.report_port),\n    lanUrls: lanUrlsForProject(project.report_port, project.report_exposure),\n  }\n}\n\nexport function disableManagedCaddy(project: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'id' | 'report_port' | 'report_exposure'>): ManagedCaddyEnableResult {\n  const paths = managedCaddyPaths(project.id)\n  if (existsSync(paths.fragmentPath)) {\n    rmSync(paths.fragmentPath, { force: true })\n  }\n\n  if (existsSync(paths.mainConfigPath)) {\n    validateManagedCaddyConfig(paths.mainConfigPath)\n    try {\n      reloadManagedCaddy(paths.mainConfigPath)\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `The project fragment was removed, but Caddy reload failed. ${formatExecError(error)} ` +\n        `Start Caddy with: ${managedCaddyStartCommand()}`,\n      )\n    }\n  }\n\n  return {\n    mainConfigPath: paths.mainConfigPath,\n    fragmentPath: paths.fragmentPath,\n    reportDir: '',\n    localUrls: localUrlsForProject(project.report_port),\n    lanUrls: lanUrlsForProject(project.report_port, project.report_exposure),\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function loadManagedCaddyStatus(project: ProjectEntry): Promise<ManagedCaddyStatus> {\n  const paths = managedCaddyPaths(project.id)\n  const installed = isCaddyInstalled()\n  const mainConfigExists = existsSync(paths.mainConfigPath)\n  const fragmentExists = existsSync(paths.fragmentPath)\n  const runningConfigText = installed ? await fetchRunningCaddyConfigText() : null\n  const running = runningConfigText !== null\n\n  let usingManagedConfig: boolean | null = null\n  if (installed && running && mainConfigExists) {\n    const managedConfigText = adaptManagedCaddyConfig(paths.mainConfigPath)\n    usingManagedConfig = stableJsonFromText(managedConfigText) === stableJsonFromText(runningConfigText)\n  }\n\n  return {\n    installed,\n    running,\n    usingManagedConfig,\n    mainConfigPath: paths.mainConfigPath,\n    mainConfigExists,\n    fragmentPath: paths.fragmentPath,\n    fragmentExists,\n    port: project.report_port,\n    exposure: project.report_exposure,\n    reportDir: projectReportDir(project),\n    localUrls: localUrlsForProject(project.report_port),\n    lanUrls: lanUrlsForProject(project.report_port, project.report_exposure),\n  }\n}\n\nexport function managedCaddyStartCommand(): string {\n  const mainConfigPath = managedCaddyPaths('placeholder').mainConfigPath\n  return `caddy run --config \"${mainConfigPath}\" --adapter caddyfile`\n}\n\nfunction managedSiteAddress(reportPort: number, exposure: ProjectEntry['report_exposure']): string {\n  if (exposure === 'lan') {\n    return `http://:${String(reportPort)}`\n  }\n  return `http://127.0.0.1:${String(reportPort)}, http://localhost:${String(reportPort)}`\n}\n\nfunction quoteForCaddyPath(value: string): string {\n  const normalized = toPosixPath(resolve(value)).replace(/\"/g, '\\\\\"')\n  return `\"${normalized}\"`\n}\n\nfunction writeTextIfChanged(path: string, content: string): void {\n  if (existsSync(path) && readFileSync(path, 'utf-8') === content) {\n    return\n  }\n  writeFileSync(path, content, 'utf-8')\n}\n\nfunction validateManagedCaddyConfig(mainConfigPath: string): void {\n  execCommand('caddy', ['adapt', '--config', mainConfigPath, '--adapter', 'caddyfile', '--validate'], {\n    allowFailure: false,\n  })\n}\n\nfunction reloadManagedCaddy(mainConfigPath: string): void {\n  execCommand('caddy', ['reload', '--config', mainConfigPath, '--adapter', 'caddyfile'])\n}\n\nfunction adaptManagedCaddyConfig(mainConfigPath: string): string {\n  return execCommand('caddy', ['adapt', '--config', mainConfigPath, '--adapter', 'caddyfile'])\n}\n\nasync function fetchRunningCaddyConfigText(): Promise<string | null> {\n  try {\n    const response = await fetch(`${CADDY_ADMIN_URL}/config/`, {\n      headers: {\n        Origin: CADDY_ADMIN_ORIGIN,\n      },\n    })\n    if (!response.ok) return null\n    return await response.text()\n  } catch {\n    return null\n  }\n}\n\nfunction stableJsonFromText(text: string | null): string | null {\n  if (text === null) return null\n  try {\n    return JSON.stringify(sortJsonKeys(JSON.parse(text)))\n  } catch {\n    return null\n  }\n}\n\nfunction sortJsonKeys(value: unknown): unknown {\n  if (Array.isArray(value)) {\n    return value.map(item => sortJsonKeys(item))\n  }\n  if (value !== null && typeof value === 'object') {\n    return Object.fromEntries(\n      Object.keys(value)\n        .sort((left, right) => left.localeCompare(right))\n        .map(key => [key, sortJsonKeys((value as Record<string, unknown>)[key])]),\n    )\n  }\n  return value\n}\n\nfunction localUrlsForProject(reportPort: number): string[] {\n  return [\n    `http://127.0.0.1:${String(reportPort)}/`,\n    `http://localhost:${String(reportPort)}/`,\n  ]\n}\n\nfunction lanUrlsForProject(\n  reportPort: number,\n  exposure: ProjectEntry['report_exposure'],\n): string[] {\n  if (exposure !== 'lan') return []\n\n  const urls = new Set<string>()\n  const interfaces = networkInterfaces()\n  for (const addresses of Object.values(interfaces)) {\n    for (const address of addresses ?? []) {\n      if (address.family !== 'IPv4' || address.internal) continue\n      urls.add(`http://${address.address}:${String(reportPort)}/`)\n    }\n  }\n  return [...urls].sort((left, right) => left.localeCompare(right))\n}\n\nfunction formatExecError(error: unknown): string {\n  if (error instanceof Error && error.message) {\n    return error.message\n  }\n  return String(error)\n}\n"],"mappings":";;;;;;;;;;;;;;AAAA,SAAS,WAAAA,gBAAe;;;ACAxB,SAAS,YAAY,WAAW,cAAc,aAAa,QAAQ,qBAAqB;AACxF,SAAS,yBAAyB;AAClC,SAAS,eAAe;AAMxB,IAAM,kBAAkB;AACxB,IAAM,qBAAqB;AAgCpB,SAAS,kBAAkB,WAAsC;AACtE,QAAM,UAAU,QAAQ,eAAe,GAAG,OAAO;AACjD,QAAM,WAAW,QAAQ,SAAS,OAAO;AACzC,SAAO;AAAA,IACL;AAAA,IACA;AAAA,IACA,gBAAgB,QAAQ,SAAS,WAAW;AAAA,IAC5C,cAAc,QAAQ,UAAU,GAAG,SAAS,QAAQ;AAAA,EACtD;AACF;AAEO,SAAS,iBAAiB,SAAyD;AACxF,SAAO,QAAQ,QAAQ,kBAAkB,UAAU,YAAY;AACjE;AAEO,SAAS,6BAAqC;AACnD,SAAO;AAAA,IACL;AAAA,IACA;AAAA,IACA;AAAA,IACA;AAAA,IACA;AAAA,IACA;AAAA,IACA;AAAA,EACF,EAAE,KAAK,IAAI;AACb;AAEO,SAAS,6BACd,SACA,WACQ;AACR,QAAM,QAAQ;AAAA,IACZ;AAAA,IACA,iBAAiB,QAAQ,EAAE;AAAA,IAC3B,mBAAmB,QAAQ,IAAI;AAAA,IAC/B,kBAAkB,OAAO,QAAQ,WAAW,CAAC;AAAA,IAC7C,sBAAsB,QAAQ,eAAe;AAAA,IAC7C;AAAA,IACA,mBAAmB,QAAQ,aAAa,QAAQ,eAAe;AAAA,IAC/D;AAAA,EACF;AAEA,MAAI,QAAQ,oBAAoB,SAAS;AACvC,UAAM,KAAK,wBAAwB;AAAA,EACrC;AAEA,QAAM,KAAK,YAAY,kBAAkB,SAAS,CAAC,EAAE;AACrD,QAAM,KAAK,eAAe;AAC1B,QAAM,KAAK,GAAG;AACd,QAAM,KAAK,EAAE;AACb,SAAO,MAAM,KAAK,IAAI;AACxB;AAEO,SAAS,mBAA4B;AAC1C,MAAI;AACF,gBAAY,SAAS,CAAC,SAAS,CAAC;AAChC,WAAO;AAAA,EACT,QAAQ;AACN,WAAO;AAAA,EACT;AACF;AAEO,SAAS,wBAAwB,WAAmB,YAAmC;AAC5F,QAAM,EAAE,UAAU,aAAa,IAAI,kBAAkB,SAAS;AAC9D,MAAI,CAAC,WAAW,QAAQ,EAAG,QAAO;AAElC,aAAW,SAAS,YAAY,QAAQ,GAAG;AACzC,QAAI,CAAC,MAAM,SAAS,QAAQ,EAAG;AAC/B,UAAM,cAAc,QAAQ,UAAU,KAAK;AAC3C,QAAI,gBAAgB,aAAc;AAElC,UAAM,OAAO,aAAa,aAAa,OAAO;AAC9C,UAAM,YAAY,KAAK,MAAM,8BAA8B;AAC3D,QAAI,CAAC,UAAW;AAEhB,UAAM,cAAc,SAAS,UAAU,CAAC,GAAI,EAAE;AAC9C,QAAI,gBAAgB,WAAY;AAEhC,UAAM,eAAe,KAAK,MAAM,4BAA4B;AAC5D,WAAO,eAAe,CAAC,GAAG,KAAK,KAAK,MAAM,QAAQ,aAAa,EAAE;AAAA,EACnE;AAEA,SAAO;AACT;AAEO,SAAS,mBAAmB,SAAiD;AAClF,QAAM,QAAQ,kBAAkB,QAAQ,EAAE;AAC1C,QAAM,oBAAoB,wBAAwB,QAAQ,IAAI,QAAQ,WAAW;AACjF,MAAI,sBAAsB,MAAM;AAC9B,UAAM,IAAI;AAAA,MACR,QAAQ,OAAO,QAAQ,WAAW,CAAC,mDAAmD,iBAAiB;AAAA,IAEzG;AAAA,EACF;AAEA,YAAU,MAAM,SAAS,EAAE,WAAW,KAAK,CAAC;AAC5C,YAAU,MAAM,UAAU,EAAE,WAAW,KAAK,CAAC;AAE7C,QAAM,YAAY,iBAAiB,OAAO;AAC1C,YAAU,WAAW,EAAE,WAAW,KAAK,CAAC;AAExC,qBAAmB,M MAAM,gBAAgB,OAAO,cAAc;AAAA,CAAI;AAC9D,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,qBAAqB,OAAO,YAAY;AAAA,CAAI;AACjE,eAAW,OAAO,OAAO,WAAW;AAClC,cAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,cAAc,GAAG;AAAA,CAAI;AAAA,IAC5C;AACA,eAAW,OAAO,OAAO,SAAS;AAChC,cAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,YAAY,GAAG;AAAA,CAAI;AAAA,IAC1C;AACA,WAAO;AAAA,EACT,SAAS,OAAgB;AACvB,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,GAAG,YAAY,KAAK,CAAC;AAAA,CAAI;AAC9C,WAAO;AAAA,EACT;AACF;AAEA,eAAsB,gBAAgB,SAA4C;AAChF,QAAM,UAAU,eAAe,QAAQ,IAAI;AAC3C,MAAI,YAAY,MAAM;AACpB,YAAQ,OAAO;AAAA,MACb;AAAA,IACF;AACA,WAAO;AAAA,EACT;AAEA,MAAI,CAAC,iBAAiB,GAAG;AACvB,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,yEAAyE;AAC9F,WAAO;AAAA,EACT;AAEA,MAAI;AACF,UAAM,SAAS,oBAAoB,OAAO;AAC1C,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,+BAA+B,QAAQ,IAAI;AAAA,CAAI;AACpE,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,gBAAgB,OAAO,cAAc;AAAA,CAAI;AAC9D,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,6BAA6B,OAAO,YAAY;AAAA,CAAI;AACzE,WAAO;AAAA,EACT,SAAS,OAAgB;AACvB,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,GAAG,YAAY,KAAK,CAAC;AAAA,CAAI;AAC9C,WAAO;AAAA,EACT;AACF;AAEA,eAAsB,eAAe,SAA4C;AAC/E,QAAM,UAAU,eAAe,QAAQ,IAAI;AAC3C,MAAI,YAAY,MAAM;AACpB,YAAQ,OAAO;AAAA,MACb;AAAA,IACF;AACA,WAAO;AAAA,EACT;AAEA,QAAM,SAAS,MAAM,uBAAuB,OAAO;AACnD,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,oBAAoB,MAAM,OAAO,SAAS,CAAC;AAAA,CAAI;AACpE,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,kBAAkB,MAAM,OAAO,OAAO,CAAC;AAAA,CAAI;AAChE,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,+BAA+B,eAAe,OAAO,kBAAkB,CAAC;AAAA,CAAI;AACjG,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,uBAAuB,MAAM,OAAO,cAAc,CAAC;AAAA,CAAI;AAC5E,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,SAAS,OAAO,OAAO,IAAI,CAAC;AAAA,CAAI;AACrD,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,aAAa,OAAO,QAAQ;AAAA,CAAI;AACrD,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,qBAAqB,OAAO,SAAS;AAAA,CAAI;AAC9D,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,gBAAgB,OAAO,cAAc;AAAA,CAAI;AAC9D,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,qBAAqB,OAAO,YAAY;AAAA,CAAI;AACjE,aAAW,OAAO,OAAO,WAAW;AAClC,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,cAAc,GAAG;AAAA,CAAI;AAAA,EAC5C;AACA,aAAW,OAAO,OAAO,SAAS;AAChC,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,YAAY,GAAG;AAAA,CAAI;AAAA,EAC1C;AACA,MAAI,CAAC,OAAO,aAAa,CAAC,OAAO,WAAW,OAAO,uBAAuB,MAAM;AAC9E,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,0BAA0B,yBAAyB,CAAC;AAAA,CAAI;AAAA,EAC/E;AACA,SAAO;AACT;AAEA,SAAS,eAAe,MAAmC;AACzD,QAAM,SAAS,WAAW;AAC1B,SAAO,mBAAmB,QAAQC,SAAQ,IAAI,CAAC;AACjD;AAEA,SAAS,MAAM,OAAwB;AACrC,SAAO,QAAQ,QAAQ;AACzB;AAEA,SAAS,eAAe,OAA+B;AACrD,MAAI,UAAU,KAAM,QAAO;AAC3B,SAAO,MAAM,KAAK;AACpB;AAEA,SAAS,YAAY,OAAwB dist\heartbeat-CAAAJJOF.js.map:1:{"version":3,"sources":["../src/heartbeat/alert.ts","../src/heartbeat/sync.ts","../src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts"],"sourcesContent":["/**\r\n * Manage ~/.memorytree/alerts.json — append, dedup, threshold, display.\r\n * Port of scripts/_alert_utils.py\r\n */\r\n\r\nimport { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, unlinkSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'\r\nimport { homedir } from 'node:os'\r\nimport { dirname, resolve } from 'node:path'\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Constants\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport const MAX_ALERTS = 100\r\n\r\nexport const ALERT_TYPES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set([\r\n  'no_remote',\r\n  'sensitive_match',\r\n  'push_failed',\r\n  'lock_held',\r\n])\r\n\r\nexport const FAILURE_THRESHOLD = 3\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Types\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport interface Alert {\r\n  readonly timestamp: string\r\n  readonly project: string\r\n  readonly type: string\r\n  readonly message: string\r\n  readonly count: number\r\n  readonly [key: string]: unknown\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Path helpers\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function alertsPath(): string {\r\n  return resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'alerts.json')\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction failureStatePath(): string {\r\n  return resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'failure_counts.json')\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Timestamp helper (self-contained — no cross-module dependency)\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nfunction utcTimestamp(): string {\r\n  return new Date().toISOString().replace(/\\.\\d{3}Z$/, 'Z')\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Read / Write alerts\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function readAlerts(): readonly Alert[] {\r\n  const path = alertsPath()\r\n  if (!existsSync(path)) {\r\n    return []\r\n  }\r\n  try {\r\n    const text = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')\r\n    const data: unknown = JSON.parse(text)\r\n    if (!Array.isArray(data)) {\r\n      return []\r\n    }\r\n    return data as Alert[]\r\n  } catch {\r\n    return []\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function writeAlert(project: string, alertType: string, message: string): void {\r\n  const alerts = readAlerts()\r\n  const now = utcTimestamp()\r\n\r\n  let found = false\r\n  const updated: Alert[] = []\r\n\r\n  for (const alert of alerts) {\r\n    if (alert.project === project && alert.type === alertType) {\r\n      updated.push({\r\n        ...alert,\r\n        timestamp: now,\r\n        message,\r\n        count: (alert.count ?? 1) + 1,\r\n      })\r\n      found = true\r\n    } else {\r\n      updated.push({ ...alert })\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  if (!found) {\r\n    updated.push({ timestamp: now, project, type: alertType, message, count: 1 })\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const trimmed = updated.length > MAX_ALERTS\r\n    ? updated.slice(updated.length - MAX_ALERTS)\r\n    : updated\r\n\r\n  saveAlerts(trimmed)\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Threshold-based writes\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function writeAlertWithThreshold(\r\n  project: string,\r\n  alertType: string,\r\n  message: string,\r\n): void {\r\n  const counts = readFailureCounts()\r\n  const key = `${project}::${alertType}`\r\n  const current = ((counts[key] as number | undefined) ?? 0) + 1\r\n  const newCounts: Record<string, number> = { ...counts, [key]: current }\r\n  saveFailureCounts(newCounts)\r\n\r\n  if (current >= FAILURE_THRESHOLD) {\r\n    writeAlert(project, alertType, message)\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function resetFailureCount(project: string, alertType: string): void {\r\n  const counts = readFailureCounts()\r\n  const key = `${project}::${alertType}`\r\n  if (key in counts) {\r\n    const newCounts: Record<string, number> = {}\r\n    for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(counts)) {\r\n      if (k !== key) {\r\n        newCounts[k] = v\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n    saveFailureCounts(newCounts)\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Clear\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function clearAlerts(): void {\r\n  const path = alertsPath()\r\n  if (existsSync(path)) {\r\n    try {\r\n      unlinkSync(path)\r\n    } catch {\r\n      // Silently ignore removal errors (matches Python behaviour)\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Display\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function formatAlertsForDisplay(alerts: readonly Record<string, unknown>[]): string {\r\n  if (alerts.length === 0) {\r\n    return ''\r\n  }\r\n  const lines: string[] = []\r\n  for (const alert of alerts) {\r\n    const count = typeof alert['count'] === 'number' ? alert['count'] : 1\r\n    const countSuffix = count > 1 ? ` (x${String(count)})` : ''\r\n    const type = typeof alert['type'] === 'string' ? alert['type'] : 'unknown'\r\n    const project = typeof alert['project'] === 'string' ? alert['project'] : '?'\r\n    const message = typeof alert['message'] === 'string' ? alert['message'] : ''\r\n    lines.push(`  [${type}] ${project}: ${message}${countSuffix}`)\r\n  }\r\n  return lines.join('\\n')\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Internal — alerts persistence\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nfunction saveAlerts(alerts: readonly Alert[]): void {\r\n  const path = alertsPath()\r\n  mkdirSync(dirname(path), { recursive: true })\r\n  writeFileSync(path, JSON.stringify(alerts, null, 2) + '\\n', 'utf-8')\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Internal — failure counts persistence\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nfunction readFailureCounts(): Record<string, number> {\r\n  const path = failureStatePath()\r\n  if (!existsSync(path)) {\r\n    return {}\r\n  }\r\n  try {\r\n    const text = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')\r\n    const data: unknown = JSON.parse(text)\r\n    if (data === null || typeof data !== 'object' || Array.isArray(data)) {\r\n      return {}\r\n    }\r\n    return data as Record<string, number>\r\n  } catch {\r\n    return {}\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction saveFailureCounts(counts: Record<string, number>): void {\r\n  const path = failureStatePath()\r\n  mkdirSync(dirname(path), { recursive: true })\r\n  writeFileSync(path, JSON.stringify(counts, null, 2) + '\\n', 'utf-8')\r\n}\r\n","import { copyFileSync, existsSync, lstatSync, mkdirSync, readdirSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'\r\nimport { dirname, join, resolve } from 'node:path'\r\n\r\nimport type { ProjectEntry } from './config.js'\r\n\r\nexport const DEV_TO_MEMORY_PATHS = [\r\n  'AGENTS.md',\r\n  'Memory/01_goals',\r\n  'Memory/02_todos',\r\n  'Memory/03_chat_logs',\r\n  'Memory/04_knowledge',\r\n  'Memory/05_archive',\r\n] as const\r\n\r\nexport const MEMORY_TO_DEV_PATHS = [\r\n  'Memory/06_transcripts',\r\n  'Memory/07_reports',\r\n] as const\r\n\r\nexport const MANAGED_REPO_PATHS = [\r\n  ...DEV_TO_MEMORY_PATHS,\r\n  ...MEMORY_TO_DEV_PATHS,\r\n] as const\r\n\r\nexport interface SyncStats {\r\n  readonly copied: number\r\n  readonly deleted: number\r\n  readonly skipped: boolean\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function projectUsesIsolatedMemoryPath(project: ProjectEntry): boolean {\r\n  return normalizeComparablePath(project.development_path) !== normalizeComparablePath(project.memory_path)\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function syncProjectContextToMemory(project: ProjectEntry): SyncStats {\r\n  if (!projectUsesIsolatedMemoryPath(project)) {\r\n    return { copied: 0, deleted: 0, skipped: true }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  return syncPathSet(project.development_path, project.memory_path, DEV_TO_MEMORY_PATHS)\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function syncProjectOutputsToDevelopment(project: ProjectEntry): SyncStats {\r\n  if (!projectUsesIsolatedMemoryPath(project)) {\r\n    return { copied: 0, deleted: 0, skipped: true }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  return syncPathSet(project.memory_path, project.development_path, MEMORY_TO_DEV_PATHS)\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction syncPathSet(\r\n  sourceRoot: string,\r\n  targetRoot: string,\r\n  relativePaths: readonly string[],\r\n): SyncStats {\r\n  let copied = 0\r\n  let deleted = 0\r\n\r\n  for (const relativePath of relativePaths) {\r\n    const stats = syncManagedPath(\r\n      resolve(sourceRoot, relativePath),\r\n      resolve(targetRoot, relativePath),\r\n    )\r\n    copied += stats.copied\r\n    deleted += stats.deleted\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  return {\r\n    copied,\r\n    deleted,\r\n    skipped: false,\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction syncManagedPath(sourcePath: string, targetPath: string): Omit<SyncStats, 'skipped'> {\r\n  if (!existsSync(sourcePath)) {\r\n    return {\r\n      copied: 0,\r\n      deleted: removeTree(targetPath),\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const sourceStats = lstatSync(sourcePath)\r\n  if (sourceStats.isDirectory()) {\r\n    return syncDirectory(sourcePath, targetPath)\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  mkdirSync(dirname(targetPath), { recursive: true })\r\n  if (existsSync(targetPath) && lstatSync(targetPath).isDirectory()) {\r\n    rmSync(targetPath, { recursive: true, force: true })\r\n  }\r\n  copyFileSync(sourcePath, targetPath)\r\n  return { copied: 1, deleted: 0 }\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction syncDirectory(sourceDir: string, targetDir: string): Omit<SyncStats, 'skipped'> {\r\n  if (existsSync(targetDir) && !lstatSync(targetDir).isDirectory()) {\r\n    rmSync(targetDir, { recursive: true, force: true })\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  mkdirSync(targetDir, { recursive: true })\r\n\r\n  let copied = 0\r\n  let deleted = 0\r\n  const sourceNames = new Set(readdirSync(sourceDir))\r\n\r\n  for (const name of sourceNames) {\r\n    const stats = syncManagedPath(join(sourceDir, name), join(targetDir, name))\r\n    copied += stats.copied\r\n    deleted += stats.deleted\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  for (const name of readdirSync(targetDir)) {\r\n    if (sourceNames.has(name)) continue\r\n    deleted += removeTree(join(targetDir, name))\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  return { copied, deleted }\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction removeTree(targetPath: string): number {\r\n  if (!existsSync(targetPath)) {\r\n    return 0\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const count = countFiles(targetPath)\r\n  rmSync(targetPath, { recursive: true, force: true })\r\n  return count\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction countFiles(targetPath: string): number {\r\n  const stats = lstatSync(targetPath)\r\n  if (!stats.isDirectory()) {\r\n    return 1\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  let total = 0\r\n  for (const name of readdirSync(targetPath)) {\r\n    total += countFiles(join(targetPath, name))\r\n  }\r\n  return total\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction normalizeComparablePath(value: string): string {\r\n  return resolve(value).replace(/\\\\/g, '/').replace(/\\/+$/, '')\r\n}\r\n","/**\r\n * MemoryTree heartbeat — single execution, stateless, idempotent.\r\n * Port of scripts/heartbeat.py\r\n */\r\n\r\nimport type { ParsedTranscript } from '../types/transcript.js'\r\nimport { discoverSourceFiles, transcriptMatchesRepo } from '../transcript/discover.js'\r\nimport { importTranscript, transcriptHasContent } from '../transcript/import.js'\r\nimport { parseTranscript } from '../transcript/parse.js'\r\nimport { slugify } from '../transcript/common.js'\r\nimport { defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot } from '../transcript/discover.js'\r\nimport type { Config, ProjectEntry, RawUploadPermission } from './config.js'\r\nimport {\r\n  findProjectForPath,\r\n  loadConfig,\r\n  noteProjectHeartbeatRun,\r\n  projectDisplayName,\r\n  projectExecutionPath,\r\n  projectIsDue,\r\n  saveConfig,\r\n} from './config.js'\r\nimport { acquireLock, releaseLock } from './lock.js'\r\nimport { resetFailureCount, writeAlert, writeAlertWithThreshold } from './alert.js'\r\nimport type { LogLevel } from './log.js'\r\nimport { getLogger, setupLogging } from './log.js'\r\nimport { MANAGED_REPO_PATHS, syncProjectContextToMemory, syncProjectOutputsToDevelopment } from './sync.js'\r\nimport { collectExtraManifestDirs } from '../report/extra-manifests.js'\r\nimport {\r\n  ensureBranchUpstream,\r\n  ensureProjectWorktree,\r\n  hasTrackingUpstream,\r\n  isProjectMemoryBranch,\r\n} from './worktree.js'\r\nimport { git } from '../utils/exec.js'\r\nimport { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'\r\nimport { basename, join, resolve } from 'node:path'\r\nimport { existsSync } from 'node:fs'\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Sensitive pattern detection\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nconst SENSITIVE_PATTERNS: readonly RegExp[] = [\r\n  /(?:api[_-]?key|apikey)\\s*[:=]\\s*\\S+/i,\r\n  /(?:password|passwd|pwd)\\s*[:=]\\s*\\S+/i,\r\n  /(?:secret|token)\\s*[:=]\\s*\\S+/i,\r\n  /(?:sk-|pk_live_|sk_live_|ghp_|gho_|glpat-)\\S{10,}/,\r\n  /Bearer\\s+\\S{20,}/i,\r\n]\r\n\r\nconst RAW_TRANSCRIPT_PREFIX = 'Memory/06_transcripts/raw/'\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Main entry point\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport interface HeartbeatRunOptions {\r\n  readonly force?: boolean\r\n  readonly root?: string\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function main(options: HeartbeatRunOptions = {}): Promise<number> {\r\n  const config = loadConfig()\r\n  setupLogging(config.log_level as LogLevel)\r\n  const logger = getLogger()\r\n\r\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\r\n    logger.info('Another heartbeat instance is running. Exiting.')\r\n    writeAlert('global', 'lock_held', 'Heartbeat exited: another instance held the lock.')\r\n    return 0\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  try {\r\n    return await runHeartbeat(config, options)\r\n  } finally {\r\n    releaseLock()\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Heartbeat orchestration\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport async function runHeartbeat(config: Config, options: HeartbeatRunOptions = {}): Promise<number> {\r\n  const logger = getLogger()\r\n  const targetProject = options.root ? findProjectForPath(config, resolve(options.root)) : null\r\n\r\n  if (config.projects.length === 0) {\r\n    logger.info('No projects registered in config.toml. Nothing to do.')\r\n    return 0\r\n  }\r\n  if (options.root && !targetProject) {\r\n    logger.warn(`No registered project matched: ${resolve(options.root)}`)\r\n    return 1\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const projects = targetProject ? [targetProject] : config.projects\r\n\r\n  logger.info(`Heartbeat started. ${projects.length} project(s) selected.`)\r\n\r\n  let nextConfig = config\r\n  let configChanged = false\r\n  const now = new Date()\r\n  const runTimestamp = now.toISOString()\r\n\r\n  for (const entry of projects) {\r\n    const projectName = projectDisplayName(entry)\r\n    const projectPath = resolve(projectExecutionPath(entry))\r\n    const heartbeatDue = options.force === true || projectIsDue(entry, now)\r\n\r\n    if (!heartbeatDue) {\r\n      logger.debug(`[${projectName}] Not due yet, skipping.`)\r\n      continue\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    try {\r\n      const worktree = ensureProjectWorktree(entry)\r\n      if (!existsSync(projectPath)) {\r\n        logger.warn(`Project path does not exist, skipping: ${projectPath}`)\r\n        continue\r\n      }\r\n\r\n      if (worktree.created) {\r\n        logger.info(`[${projectName}] Created worktree on branch '${worktree.branch}'.`)\r\n      }\r\n\r\n      const contextSync = syncProjectContextToMemory(entry)\r\n      if (!contextSync.skipped) {\r\n        logger.info(\r\n          `[${projectName}] Synced context to ${worktree.branch} ` +\r\n          `(copied ${contextSync.copied}, deleted ${contextSync.deleted}).`,\r\n        )\r\n      }\r\n\r\n      const extraManifestDirs = collectExtraManifestDirs(projectPath)\r\n      await processProject(config, projectPath, projectName, entry, extraManifestDirs)\r\n      nextConfig = noteProjectHeartbeatRun(nextConfig, entry.id, runTimestamp)\r\n      configChanged = true\r\n\r\n      const outputSync = syncProjectOutputsToDevelopment(entry)\r\n      if (!outputSync.skipped) {\r\n        logger.info(\r\n          `[${projectName}] Synced outputs back to development directory ` +\r\n          `(copied ${outputSync.copied}, deleted ${outputSync.deleted}).`,\r\n        )\r\n      }\r\n    } catch (err: unknown) {\r\n      logger.exception(`Error processing project: ${projectPath}`, err)\r\n      writeAlertWithThreshold(\r\n        toPosixPath(projectPath),\r\n        'push_failed',\r\n        `Heartbeat error for project: ${basename(projectPath)}`,\r\n      )\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  if (configChanged) {\r\n    saveConfig(nextConfig)\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  logger.info('Heartbeat finished.')\r\n  return 0\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Per-project processing\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport async function processProject(\r\n  config: Config,\r\n  projectPath: string,\r\n  projectName: string,\r\n  project?: ProjectEntry,\r\n  extraManifestDirs?: string[],\r\n): Promise<void> {\r\n  const logger = getLogger()\r\n  const repoSlug = slugify(projectName, 'project')\r\n  const globalRoot = defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot()\r\n  const branch = currentBranch(projectPath)\r\n  const mirrorToRepo = isDedicatedMemorytreeBranch(branch, project)\r\n  const autoPush = project?.auto_push ?? config.auto_push ?? true\r\n  const generateReport = project?.generate_report ?? config.generate_report ?? false\r\n  const aiSummaryModel = project?.ai_summary_model ?? config.ai_summary_model ?? 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'\r\n  const locale = project?.locale ?? config.locale ?? 'en'\r\n  const ghPagesBranch = project?.gh_pages_branch ?? config.gh_pages_branch ?? ''\r\n  const cname = project?.cname ?? config.cname ?? ''\r\n  const webhookUrl = project?.webhook_url ?? config.webhook_url ?? ''\r\n  const reportBaseUrl = project?.report_base_url ?? config.report_base_url ?? ''\r\n  const rawUploadPermission = project?.raw_upload_permission ?? 'not-set'\r\n\r\n  if (!mirrorToRepo) {\r\n    logger.warn(\r\n      `[${projectName}] Current branch '${branch}' is not a dedicated MemoryTree branch. ` +\r\n      'Importing to the global archive only.',\r\n    )\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const discovered = discoverSourceFiles()\r\n  let importedCount = 0\r\n\r\n  for (const [client, source] of discovered) {\r\n    let parsed: ParsedTranscript\r\n    try {\r\n      parsed = parseTranscript(client, source)\r\n    } catch {\r\n      logger.debug(`Failed to parse ${source}, skipping.`)\r\n      continue\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (!transcriptHasContent(parsed)) continue\r\n    if (!transcriptMatchesRepo(parsed, projectPath, repoSlug)) continue\r\n\r\n    scanSensitive(parsed, projectPath)\r\n\r\n    try {\r\n      await importTranscript(parsed, projectPath, globalRoot, repoSlug, rawUploadPermission, mirrorToRepo)\r\n      importedCount++\r\n    } catch {\r\n      logger.exception(`Failed to import transcript: ${source}`)\r\n    }\…103335 tokens ,CAACH,YAAW,MAAM,EAAG;AAEzB,MAAI;AACJ,MAAI;AACF,cAAU,YAAY,MAAM;AAAA,EAC9B,QAAQ;AACN;AAAA,EACF;AAEA,aAAW,SAAS,SAAS;AAC3B,QAAI;AACF,aAAOF,MAAK,QAAQ,KAAK,GAAG,EAAE,WAAW,MAAM,OAAO,KAAK,CAAC;AAAA,IAC9D,QAAQ;AAAA,IAER;AAAA,EACF;AACF;AAEA,SAAS,qBAAqB,SAAyB;AACrD,QAAM,QAAQ,QAAQ,MAAM,aAAa;AACzC,SAAO,QAAQ,CAAC,GAAG,KAAK,KAAK;AAC/B;AAMO,SAAS,gBAAgB,MAAc,OAAqB;AACjE,QAAM,gBAAgBA,MAAK,MAAM,YAAY;AAC7C,MAAI,WAAW;AACf,MAAIE,YAAW,aAAa,GAAG;AAC7B,QAAI;AACF,iBAAWE,cAAa,eAAe,OAAO;AAAA,IAChD,QAAQ;AACN;AAAA,IACF;AAAA,EACF;AAEA,QAAM,QAAQ,SAAS,QAAQ,SAAS,IAAI,EAAE,MAAM,IAAI;AACxD,MAAI,MAAM,KAAK,OAAK,EAAE,KAAK,MAAM,MAAM,KAAK,CAAC,EAAG;AAGhD,QAAM,SAAS,SAAS,SAAS,IAAI,IAAI,QAAQ,OAAO,OAAO,QAAQ;AACvE,MAAI;AACF,mBAAe,eAAe,QAAQ,OAAO;AAAA,EAC/C,QAAQ;AAAA,EAER;AACF;","names":["existsSync","mkdirSync","readFileSync","writeFileSync","basename","join","content","content","formatTemplate","transcriptHref","content","content","content","content","content","existsSync","readFileSync","basename","join","existsSync","mkdirSync","readFileSync","writeFileSync","join","join","mkdirSync","existsSync","writeFileSync","readFileSync","basename"]}
