C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow\dist\cmd-daemon-GSFIGIE3.js.map:1:{"version":3,"sources":["../src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts"],"sourcesContent":["/**\r\n * CLI: memorytree daemon install|uninstall|run-once|watch|status\r\n * Port of scripts/memorytree_daemon.py\r\n */\r\n\r\nimport { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, unlinkSync } from 'node:fs'\r\nimport { homedir } from 'node:os'\r\nimport { basename, dirname, resolve } from 'node:path'\r\nimport { platform } from 'node:process'\r\nimport { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'\r\n\r\nimport {\r\n  DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH,\r\n  DEFAULT_RAW_UPLOAD_PERMISSION,\r\n  configPath,\r\n  findProjectForPath,\r\n  intervalToSeconds,\r\n  loadConfig,\r\n  normalizeRawUploadPermission,\r\n  saveConfig,\r\n  upsertProject,\r\n} from '../heartbeat/config.js'\r\nimport { readLockPid } from '../heartbeat/lock.js'\r\nimport {\n  describePushRemote,\n  defaultProjectWorktreePath,\n  defaultProjectWorktreeBranch,\n  ensureBranchUpstream,\n  ensureProjectWorktree,\n  isValidWorktreeBranchName,\n  redactRemoteUrl,\n} from '../heartbeat/worktree.js'\nimport { execCommand } from '../utils/exec.js'\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Constants\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nconst TASK_NAME = 'MemoryTree Heartbeat'\r\nconst LAUNCHD_LABEL = 'com.memorytree.heartbeat'\r\nconst QUICK_START_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = '5m'\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Subcommands\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function cmdInstall(options: { interval?: string; autoPush?: string }): number {\r\n  let config = loadConfig()\r\n\r\n  if (options.interval) {\r\n    config = { ...config, heartbeat_interval: options.interval }\r\n  }\r\n  if (options.autoPush) {\r\n    config = { ...config, auto_push: options.autoPush === 'true' }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  saveConfig(config)\r\n  const seconds = intervalToSeconds(config.heartbeat_interval)\r\n  const scriptPath = heartbeatScriptPath()\r\n\r\n  const sys = platform\r\n  if (sys === 'linux') return installCron(scriptPath, seconds)\r\n  if (sys === 'darwin') return installLaunchd(scriptPath, seconds)\r\n  if (sys === 'win32') return installSchtasks(scriptPath, seconds)\r\n\r\n  process.stderr.write(`Unsupported platform: ${sys}\\n`)\r\n  return 1\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function cmdUninstall(): number {\r\n  const sys = platform\r\n  if (sys === 'linux') return uninstallCron()\r\n  if (sys === 'darwin') return uninstallLaunchd()\r\n  if (sys === 'win32') return uninstallSchtasks()\r\n\r\n  process.stderr.write(`Unsupported platform: ${sys}\\n`)\r\n  return 1\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function cmdRunOnce(options: { root?: string; force?: boolean } = {}): Promise<number> {\r\n  const { main } = await import('../heartbeat/heartbeat.js')\r\n  return main(options)\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function cmdWatch(options: { interval?: string }): Promise<number> {\r\n  const config = loadConfig()\r\n  const intervalStr = options.interval ?? config.heartbeat_interval\r\n  const seconds = intervalToSeconds(intervalStr)\r\n  const { main } = await import('../heartbeat/heartbeat.js')\r\n\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Watch mode: running heartbeat every ${seconds}s. Press Ctrl+C to stop.\\n`)\r\n  try {\r\n    // eslint-disable-next-line no-constant-condition\r\n    while (true) {\r\n      await main()\r\n      await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, seconds * 1000))\r\n    }\r\n  } catch {\r\n    process.stdout.write('\\nWatch mode stopped.\\n')\r\n  }\r\n  return 0\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function cmdStatus(): number {\r\n  const registered = isHeartbeatRegistered()\r\n\r\n  const sys = platform\r\n  const platformName = sys === 'darwin' ? 'Darwin' : sys === 'win32' ? 'Windows' : 'Linux'\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Platform:   ${platformName}\\n`)\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Registered: ${registered ? 'yes' : 'no'}\\n`)\r\n\r\n  const pid = readLockPid()\r\n  if (pid !== null) {\r\n    process.stdout.write(`Lock:       held by PID ${pid}\\n`)\r\n  } else {\r\n    process.stdout.write('Lock:       not held\\n')\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  if (existsSync(configPath())) {\r\n    const config = loadConfig()\r\n    process.stdout.write(`Interval:   ${config.heartbeat_interval}\\n`)\r\n    process.stdout.write(`Auto-push:  ${config.auto_push}\\n`)\r\n    process.stdout.write(`Projects:   ${config.projects.length}\\n`)\r\n  } else {\r\n    process.stdout.write('Config:     not found (using defaults)\\n')\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  return 0\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function isHeartbeatRegistered(): boolean {\r\n  const sys = platform\r\n  if (sys === 'linux') return isCronRegistered()\r\n  if (sys === 'darwin') return isLaunchdRegistered()\r\n  if (sys === 'win32') return isSchtasksRegistered()\r\n  return false\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function cmdRegisterProject(options: {\r\n  root: string\r\n  name?: string\r\n  worktree?: string\r\n  branch?: string\r\n  quickStart?: boolean\r\n  heartbeatInterval?: string\r\n  autoPush?: string\r\n  generateReport?: string\r\n  reportPort?: string\r\n  rawUploadPermission?: string\r\n}): number {\r\n  const root = resolve(options.root)\r\n  const config = loadConfig()\r\n  const existingProject = findProjectForPath(config, root)\r\n\r\n  if (options.quickStart && options.branch !== undefined) {\r\n    process.stderr.write('Quick Start uses the default memorytree branch. Omit --branch to customize it.\\n')\r\n    return 1\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const requestedBranch = options.quickStart\r\n    ? existingProject?.memory_branch ?? defaultProjectWorktreeBranch()\r\n    : options.branch ?? existingProject?.memory_branch ?? DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH\r\n  if (!isValidWorktreeBranchName(requestedBranch)) {\r\n    process.stderr.write(`Invalid MemoryTree branch name: ${requestedBranch}\\n`)\r\n    return 1\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const heartbeatInterval = options.quickStart\r\n    ? existingProject?.heartbeat_interval ?? QUICK_START_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL\r\n    : options.heartbeatInterval ?? existingProject?.heartbeat_interval ?? config.heartbeat_interval\r\n  const autoPush = options.quickStart\r\n    ? existingProject?.auto_push ?? true\r\n    : parseOptionalBoolean(options.autoPush) ?? existingProject?.auto_push ?? config.auto_push\r\n  const generateReport = options.quickStart\r\n    ? existingProject?.generate_report ?? true\r\n    : parseOptionalBoolean(options.generateReport) ?? existingProject?.generate_report ?? config.generate_report\r\n  const rawUploadPermission = options.quickStart\r\n    ? existingProject?.raw_upload_permission ?? DEFAULT_RAW_UPLOAD_PERMISSION\r\n    : options.rawUploadPermission !== undefined\r\n      ? parseRawUploadPermissionOption(options.rawUploadPermission)\r\n      : existingProject?.raw_upload_permission ?? DEFAULT_RAW_UPLOAD_PERMISSION\r\n  if (rawUploadPermission === null) {\r\n    process.stderr.write('Invalid raw upload permission. Use not-set, approved, or denied.\\n')\r\n    return 1\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const requestedPort = options.reportPort ? parseInt(options.reportPort, 10) : NaN\r\n  const reportPort = Number.isInteger(requestedPort) && requestedPort > 0 && requestedPort <= 65535\r\n    ? requestedPort\r\n    : existingProject?.report_port ?? config.report_port\r\n\r\n  const updated = upsertProject(config, root, {\r\n    name: options.name ?? existingProject?.name ?? basename(root),\r\n    development_path: root,\r\n    memory_path: options.worktree\r\n      ? resolve(options.worktree)\r\n      : existingProject?.memory_path ?? defaultProjectWorktreePath(root),\r\n    memory_branch: requestedBranch,\r\n    heartbeat_interval: heartbeatInterval,\r\n    auto_push: autoPush,\r\n    generate_report: generateReport,\r\n    report_port: reportPort,\r\n    raw_upload_permission: rawUploadPermission,\r\n  })\r\n\r\n  const project = findProjectForPath(updated, root)\r\n  if (!project) {\r\n    process.stderr.write(`Failed to register project: ${root}\\n`)\r\n    return 1\r\n  }\r\n\n  const worktree = ensureProjectWorktree(project)\n  saveConfig(updated)\n  const pushRemote = describePushRemote(project.memory_path)\n  let upstream: ReturnType<typeof ensureBranchUpstream> | null = null\n  let upstreamError = ''\n  if (project.auto_push) {\n    try {\n      upstream = ensureBranchUpstream(project.memory_path, worktree.branch)\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\r\n      upstreamError = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Registered project: ${project.name}\\n`)\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Development path: ${project.development_path}\\n`)\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Memory path: ${project.memory_path}\\n`)\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Memory branch: ${project.memory_branch}\\n`)\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Heartbeat interval: ${project.heartbeat_interval}\\n`)\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Auto-push: ${project.auto_push}\\n`)\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Generate report: ${project.generate_report}\\n`)\n  process.stdout.write(`Raw upload permission: ${project.raw_upload_permission}\\n`)\n  process.stdout.write(`Worktree branch: ${worktree.branch}\\n`)\n  process.stdout.write(`Worktree created: ${worktree.created ? 'yes' : 'no'}\\n`)\n  process.stdout.write(`Push remote: ${pushRemote.remote ?? 'none'}\\n`)\n  if (pushRemote.pushUrl !== null) {\n    process.stdout.write(`Push URL: ${redactRemoteUrl(pushRemote.pushUrl)}\\n`)\n  }\n  if (upstreamError) {\n    process.stderr.write(`Upstream configured: failed (${upstreamError})\\n`)\n  } else if (upstream === null) {\n    process.stdout.write('Upstream configured: skipped (auto_push disabled)\\n')\n  } else if (upstream.remote === null) {\n    process.stdout.write('Upstream configured: no remote available\\n')\n  } else {\n    process.stdout.write(`Upstream configured: ${upstream.created ? 'yes' : 'already'} (${upstream.remote}/${worktree.branch})\\n`)\n    if (upstream.usedFallback && upstream.pushUrl !== null) {\n      process.stdout.write(`Push fallback used: ${redactRemoteUrl(upstream.pushUrl)}\\n`)\n    }\n  }\n  return upstreamError ? 1 : 0\n}\n\r\nfunction parseRawUploadPermissionOption(value: string): 'not-set' | 'approved' | 'denied' | null {\r\n  const normalized = normalizeRawUploadPermission(value, DEFAULT_RAW_UPLOAD_PERMISSION)\r\n  return normalized === DEFAULT_RAW_UPLOAD_PERMISSION && value.trim().toLowerCase() !== DEFAULT_RAW_UPLOAD_PERMISSION\r\n    ? null\r\n    : normalized\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function cmdQuickStart(options: {\r\n  root: string\r\n  name?: string\r\n}): Promise<number> {\r\n  const root = resolve(options.root)\r\n  const config = loadConfig()\r\n  const quickStartSeconds = intervalToSeconds(QUICK_START_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL)\r\n  const schedulerIntervalSeconds = registeredHeartbeatIntervalSeconds()\r\n  const effectiveSchedulerSeconds = schedulerIntervalSeconds ?? intervalToSeconds(config.heartbeat_interval)\r\n  const effectiveSchedulerLabel = schedulerIntervalSeconds === null\r\n    ? config.heartbeat_interval\r\n    : formatIntervalSeconds(schedulerIntervalSeconds)\r\n\r\n  process.stdout.write('Quick Start: prepare scheduler, register this repository in the single-source memory flow, and run one sync now.\\n')\r\n\r\n  if (!isHeartbeatRegistered()) {\r\n    process.stdout.write('Step 1/3: installing heartbeat scheduler with recommended defaults.\\n')\r\n    const installResult = cmdInstall({ interval: QUICK_START_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL, autoPush: 'true' })\r\n    if (installResult !== 0) {\r\n      return installResult\r\n    }\r\n  } else if (effectiveSchedulerSeconds > quickStartSeconds) {\r\n    process.stdout.write(\r\n      `Step 1/3: heartbeat scheduler is registered at ${effectiveSchedulerLabel}; ` +\r\n      `reinstalling it to ${QUICK_START_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL} so Quick Start can run on time.\\n`,\r\n    )\r\n    const uninstallResult = cmdUninstall()\r\n    if (uninstallResult !== 0) {\r\n      return uninstallResult\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const installResult = cmdInstall({\r\n      interval: QUICK_START_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL,\r\n      autoPush: config.auto_push ? 'true' : 'false',\r\n    })\r\n    if (installResult !== 0) {\r\n      return installResult\r\n    }\r\n  } else {\r\n    process.stdout.write(\r\n      `Step 1/3: heartbeat scheduler already registered at ${effectiveSchedulerLabel}; keeping the existing install.\\n`,\r\n    )\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  process.stdout.write('Step 2/3: registering the current repository with Quick Start settings.\\n')\r\n  const registerOptions: Parameters<typeof cmdRegisterProject>[0] = {\r\n    root,\r\n    quickStart: true,\r\n  }\r\n  if (options.name !== undefined) {\r\n    registerOptions.name = options.name\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const registerResult = cmdRegisterProject(registerOptions)\r\n  if (registerResult !== 0) {\r\n    return registerResult\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  process.stdout.write('Step 3/3: running one immediate heartbeat sync.\\n')\r\n  return await cmdRunOnce({\r\n    root,\r\n    force: true,\r\n  })\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function registeredHeartbeatIntervalSeconds(): number | null {\r\n  const sys = platform\r\n  if (sys === 'linux') return cronRegisteredIntervalSeconds()\r\n  if (sys === 'darwin') return launchdRegisteredIntervalSeconds()\r\n  if (sys === 'win32') return schtasksRegisteredIntervalSeconds()\r\n  return null\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Linux (cron)\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nfunction installCron(scriptPath: string, seconds: number): number {\r\n  if (isCronRegistered()) {\r\n    process.stderr.write(\"Heartbeat is already registered in cron. Use 'uninstall' first.\\n\")\r\n    return 1\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const minutes = Math.max(1, Math.floor(seconds / 60))\r\n  const logDir = resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'logs')\r\n  mkdirSync(logDir, { recursive: true })\r\n\r\n  const cronLine = `*/${minutes} * * * * node \"${scriptPath}\" daemon run-once >> \"${resolve(logDir, 'heartbeat-cron.log')}\" 2>&1 # memorytree`\r\n  const existing = getCrontab()\r\n  const newCrontab = existing.trim()\r\n    ? existing.trimEnd() + '\\n' + cronLine + '\\n'\r\n    : cronLine + '\\n'\r\n\r\n  try {\r\n    const tmpFile = resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', '.crontab.tmp')\r\n    writeFileSync(tmpFile, newCrontab)\r\n    execCommand('crontab', [tmpFile])\r\n    unlinkSync(tmpFile)\r\n  } catch {\r\n    process.stderr.write('Failed to install cron job.\\n')\r\n    return 1\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Heartbeat registered in cron (every ${minutes}m).\\n`)\r\n  return 0\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction uninstallCron(): number {\r\n  const existing = getCrontab()\r\n  const filtered = existing.split('\\n').filter(line => !line.includes('memorytree')).join('\\n') + '\\n'\r\n  try {\r\n    const tmpFile = resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', '.crontab.tmp')\r\n    mkdirSync(dirname(tmpFile), { recursive: true })\r\n    writeFileSync(tmpFile, filtered)\r\n    execCommand('crontab', [tmpFile])\r\n    unlinkSync(tmpFile)\r\n  } catch {\r\n    // best effort\r\n  }\r\n  process.stdout.write('Heartbeat removed from cron.\\n')\r\n  return 0\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function isCronRegistered(): boolean {\r\n  return getCrontab().includes('memorytree')\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction getCrontab(): string {\r\n  try {\r\n    return execCommand('crontab', ['-l'], { allowFailure: true })\r\n  } catch {\r\n    return ''\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction cronRegisteredIntervalSeconds(): number | null {\r\n  const line = getCrontab()\r\n    .split(/\\r?\\n/)\r\n    .find(candidate => candidate.includes('memorytree'))\r\n  if (!line) return null\r\n\r\n  const match = line.match(/^\\*\\/(\\d+)\\s+\\*\\s+\\*\\s+\\*\\s+\\*.*#\\s*memorytree\\s*$/)\r\n  if (!match) return null\r\n\r\n  const minutes = parseInt(match[1]!, 10)\r\n  return Number.isInteger(minutes) && minutes > 0 ? minutes * 60 : null\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// macOS (launchd)\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nfunction launchdPlistPath(): string {\r\n  return resolve(homedir(), 'Library', 'LaunchAgents', `${LAUNCHD_LABEL}.plist`)\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction installLaunchd(scriptPath: string, seconds: number): number {\r\n  const plistPath = launchdPlistPath()\r\n  if (existsSync(plistPath)) {\r\n    process.stderr.write(\"Heartbeat plist already exists. Use 'uninstall' first.\\n\")\r\n    return 1\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const logDir = resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'logs')\r\n  mkdirSync(logDir, { recursive: true })\r\n\r\n  const plistContent = `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\r\n<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC \"-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN\" \"http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd\">\r\n<plist version=\"1.0\">\r\n<dict>\r\n    <key>Label</key>\r\n    <string>${LAUNCHD_LABEL}</string>\r\n    <key>ProgramArguments</key>\r\n    <array>\r\n        <string>node</string>\r\n        <string>${scriptPath}</string>\r\n        <string>daemon</string>\r\n        <string>run-once</string>\r\n    </array>\r\n    <key>StartInterval</key>\r\n    <integer>${seconds}</integer>\r\n    <key>StandardOutPath</key>\r\n    <string>${resolve(logDir, 'heartbeat-launchd.log')}</string>\r\n    <key>StandardErrorPath</key>\r\n    <string>${resolve(logDir, 'heartbeat-launchd.log')}</string>\r\n</dict>\r\n</plist>\r\n`\r\n  mkdirSync(dirname(plistPath), { recursive: true })\r\n  writeFileSync(plistPath, plistContent, 'utf-8')\r\n\r\n  try {\r\n    execCommand('launchctl', ['load', plistPath])\r\n  } catch {\r\n    process.stderr.write('Failed to load plist.\\n')\r\n    return 1\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Heartbeat registered via launchd (every ${seconds}s).\\n`)\r\n  return 0\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction uninstallLaunchd(): number {\r\n  const plistPath = launchdPlistPath()\r\n  if (existsSync(plistPath)) {\r\n    try { execCommand('launchctl', ['unload', plistPath], { allowFailure: true }) } catch { /* ignore */ }\r\n    try { unlinkSync(plistPath) } catch { /* ignore */ }\r\n  }\r\n  process.stdout.write('Heartbeat removed from launchd.\\n')\r\n  return 0\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function isLaunchdRegistered(): boolean {\r\n  return existsSync(launchdPlistPath())\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction launchdRegisteredIntervalSeconds(): number | null {\r\n  const plistPath = launchdPlistPath()\r\n  if (!existsSync(plistPath)) return null\r\n\r\n  try {\r\n    const text = readFileSync(plistPath, 'utf-8')\r\n    const match = text.match(/<key>StartInterval<\\/key>\\s*<integer>(\\d+)<\\/integer>/i)\r\n    if (!match) return null\r\n\r\n    const seconds = parseInt(match[1]!, 10)\r\n    return Number.isInteger(seconds) && seconds > 0 ? seconds : null\r\n  } catch {\r\n    return null\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Windows (Task Scheduler)\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\n// Write a VBScript launcher that runs node silently (no console window).\r\n// wscript.exe /B runs in batch mode (suppresses script error popups).\r\n// VBScript Run(..., 0, False): 0 = SW_HIDE hides the window; False = fire-and-forget.\r\n// Written as UTF-16 LE with BOM so wscript.exe handles non-ASCII paths (e.g. Chinese\r\n// usernames) correctly on all Windows versions.\r\nexport function vbsLauncherPath(): string {\r\n  return resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'heartbeat-launcher.vbs')\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function writeVbsLauncher(scriptPath: string): string {\r\n  const vbsPath = vbsLauncherPath()\r\n  mkdirSync(dirname(vbsPath), { recursive: true })\r\n  // Inside VBScript strings, double-quote is escaped as \"\"\r\n  const escapedScript = scriptPath.replace(/\"/g, '\"\"')\r\n  // Use the full node binary path so the launcher works even when Task Scheduler's\r\n  // PATH differs from the user's shell (e.g. nvm/fnm environments).\r\n  const escapedNode = process.execPath.replace(/\"/g, '\"\"')\r\n  const content = [\r\n    'Set WshShell = CreateObject(\"WScript.Shell\")',\r\n    `WshShell.Run \"\"\"${escapedNode}\"\" \"\"${escapedScript}\"\" daemon run-once\", 0, False`,\r\n  ].join('\\r\\n') + '\\r\\n'\r\n  // UTF-16 LE BOM: recognised by wscript.exe on all Windows versions and preserves\r\n  // non-ASCII characters (e.g. Chinese home directory paths) without corruption.\r\n  const buf = Buffer.concat([Buffer.from([0xff, 0xfe]), Buffer.from(content, 'utf16le')])\r\n  writeFileSync(vbsPath, buf)\r\n  return vbsPath\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction installSchtasks(scriptPath: string, seconds: number): number {\r\n  if (isSchtasksRegistered()) {\r\n    process.stderr.write(\"Heartbeat is already registered in Task Scheduler. Use 'uninstall' first.\\n\")\r\n    return 1\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const minutes = Math.max(1, Math.floor(seconds / 60))\r\n  const vbsPath = writeVbsLauncher(scriptPath)\r\n  const trCommand = `wscript.exe /B \"${vbsPath}\"`\r\n\r\n  try {\r\n    execCommand('schtasks', [\r\n      '/create', '/tn', TASK_NAME, '/sc', 'minute', '/mo', String(minutes),\r\n      '/tr', trCommand, '/f',\r\n    ])\r\n  } catch {\r\n    // schtasks failed — remove the launcher file we just wrote to keep state clean\r\n    try { unlinkSync(vbsPath) } catch { /* ignore */ }\r\n    process.stderr.write('Failed to create scheduled task.\\n')\r\n    return 1\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  process.stdout.write(`Heartbeat registered in Task Scheduler (every ${minutes}m).\\n`)\r\n  return 0\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction uninstallSchtasks(): number {\r\n  try {\r\n    execCommand('schtasks', ['/delete', '/tn', TASK_NAME, '/f'], { allowFailure: true })\r\n  } catch {\r\n    // best effort\r\n  }\r\n  const vbsPath = vbsLauncherPath()\r\n  if (existsSync(vbsPath)) {\r\n    try { unlinkSync(vbsPath) } catch { /* ignore */ }\r\n  }\r\n  process.stdout.write('Heartbeat removed from Task Scheduler.\\n')\r\n  return 0\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function isSchtasksRegistered(): boolean {\r\n  try {\r\n    execCommand('schtasks', ['/query', '/tn', TASK_NAME])\r\n    return true\r\n  } catch {\r\n    return false\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction schtasksRegisteredIntervalSeconds(): number | null {\r\n  try {\r\n    const xml = execCommand('schtasks', ['/query', '/tn', TASK_NAME, '/xml'], { allowFailure: true })\r\n    if (!xml.trim()) return null\r\n\r\n    const match = xml.match(/<Interval>([^<]+)<\\/Interval>/i)\r\n    if (!match) return null\r\n\r\n    return parseIsoDurationToSeconds(match[1]!)\r\n  } catch {\r\n    return null\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Helpers\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n\r\nexport function heartbeatScriptPath(): string {\r\n  // Use process.argv[1] which is the actual script being executed,\r\n  // more reliable than import.meta.url which varies between tsx and bundled output\r\n  const scriptArg = process.argv[1] ?? ''\r\n  if (scriptArg && scriptArg.endsWith('cli.js')) {\r\n    return resolve(scriptArg)\r\n  }\r\n  return fallbackHeartbeatScriptPath(import.meta.url)\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function fallbackHeartbeatScriptPath(moduleUrl: string): string {\r\n  const moduleDir = dirname(fileURLToPath(moduleUrl))\r\n  if (basename(moduleDir).toLowerCase() === 'dist') {\r\n    return resolve(moduleDir, 'cli.js')\r\n  }\r\n  return resolve(moduleDir, '..', '..', 'dist', 'cli.js')\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction parseOptionalBoolean(value: string | undefined): boolean | undefined {\r\n  if (value === undefined) return undefined\r\n  if (value === 'true') return true\r\n  if (value === 'false') return false\r\n  return undefined\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction parseIsoDurationToSeconds(value: string): number | null {\r\n  const match = value.trim().match(/^P(?:(\\d+)D)?(?:T(?:(\\d+)H)?(?:(\\d+)M)?(?:(\\d+)S)?)?$/i)\r\n  if (!match) return null\r\n\r\n  const days = parseInt(match[1] ?? '0', 10)\r\n  const hours = parseInt(match[2] ?? '0', 10)\r\n  const minutes = parseInt(match[3] ?? '0', 10)\r\n  const seconds = parseInt(match[4] ?? '0', 10)\r\n  const total = (days * 86400) + (hours * 3600) + (minutes * 60) + seconds\r\n  return total > 0 ? total : null\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction formatIntervalSeconds(seconds: number): string {\r\n  if (seconds % 3600 === 0) return `${String(seconds / 3600)}h`\r\n  if (seconds % 60 === 0) return `${String(seconds / 60)}m`\r\n  return `${String(seconds)}s`\r\n}\r\n"],"mappings":";;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;AAKA,SAAS,YAAY,WAAW,cAAc,eAAe,kBAAkB;AAC/E,SAAS,eAAe;AACxB,SAAS,UAAU,SAAS,eAAe;AAC3C,SAAS,gBAAgB;AACzB,SAAS,qBAAqB;AA6B9B,IAAM,YAAY;AAClB,IAAM,gBAAgB;AACtB,IAAM,iCAAiC;AAMhC,SAAS,WAAW,SAA2D;AACpF,MAAI,SAAS,WAAW;AAExB,MAAI,QAAQ,UAAU;AACpB,aAAS,EAAE,GAAG,QAAQ,oBAAoB,QAAQ,SAAS;AAAA,EAC7D;AACA,MAAI,QAAQ,UAAU;AACpB,aAAS,EAAE,GAAG,QAAQ,WAAW,QAAQ,aAAa,OAAO;AAAA,EAC/D;AAEA,aAAW,MAAM;AACjB,QAAM,UAAU,kBAAkB,OAAO,kBAAkB;AAC3D,QAAM,aAAa,oBAAoB;AAEvC,QAAM,MAAM;AACZ,MAAI,QAAQ,QAAS,QAAO,YAAY,YAAY,OAAO;AAC3D,MAAI,QAAQ,SAAU,QAAO,eAAe,YAAY,OAAO;AAC/D,MAAI,QAAQ,QAAS,QAAO,gBAAgB,YAAY,OAAO;AAE/D,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,yBAAyB,GAAG;AAAA,CAAI;AACrD,SAAO;AACT;AAEO,SAAS,eAAuB;AACrC,QAAM,MAAM;AACZ,MAAI,QAAQ,QAAS,QAAO,cAAc;AAC1C,MAAI,QAAQ,SAAU,QAAO,iBAAiB;AAC9C,MAAI,QAAQ,QAAS,QAAO,kBAAkB;AAE9C,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,yBAAyB,GAAG;AAAA,CAAI;AACrD,SAAO;AACT;AAEA,eAAsB,WAAW,UAA8C,CAAC,GAAoB;AAClG,QAAM,EAAE,KAAK,IAAI,MAAM,OAAO,yBAA2B;AACzD,SAAO,KAAK,OAAO;AACrB;AAEA,eAAsB,SAAS,SAAiD;AAC9E,QAAM,SAAS,WAAW;AAC1B,QAAM,cAAc,QAAQ,YAAY,OAAO;AAC/C,QAAM,UAAU,kBAAkB,WAAW;AAC7C,QAAM,EAAE,KAAK,IAAI,MAAM,OAAO,yBAA2B;AAEzD,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,uCAAuC,OAAO;AAAA,CAA4B;AAC/F,MAAI;AAEF,WAAO,MAAM;AACX,YAAM,KAAK;AACX,YAAM,IAAI,QAAQ,OAAK,WAAW,GAAG,UAAU,GAAI,CAAC;AAAA,IACtD;AAAA,EACF,QAAQ;AACN,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,yBAAyB;AAAA,EAChD;AACA,SAAO;AACT;AAEO,SAAS,YAAoB;AAClC,QAAM,aAAa,sBAAsB;AAEzC,QAAM,MAAM;AACZ,QAAM,eAAe,QAAQ,WAAW,WAAW,QAAQ,UAAU,YAAY;AACjF,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,eAAe,YAAY;AAAA,CAAI;AACpD,UAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,eAAe,aAAa,QAAQ,IAAI;AAAA,CAAI;AAEjE,QAAM,MAAM,YAAY;AACxB,MAAI,QAAQ,MAAM;AAChB,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,2BAA2B,GAAG;AAAA,CAAI;AAAA,EACzD,OAAO;AACL,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,wBAAwB;AAAA,EAC/C;AAEA,MAAI,WAAW,WAAW,CAAC,GAAG;AAC5B,UAAM,SAAS,WAAW;AAC1B,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,eAAe,OAAO,kBAAkB;AAAA,CAAI;AACjE,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,eAAe,OAAO,SAAS;AAAA,CAAI;AACxD,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,eAAe,OAAO,SAAS,MAAM;AAAA,CAAI;AAAA,EAChE,OAAO;AACL,YAAQ,OAAO,MAAM,0CAA0C;AAAA,EACjE;AAEA,SAAO;AACT;AAEO,SAAS,wBAAiC;AAC/C,QAAM,MAAM;AACZ,MAAI,QAAQ assistant #784 final_answer 03:09:52

原因其实很简单：你这次做的是“重新编译并重新链接当前目录”，不是“把当前目录同步到 GitHub 最新提交”。

看这张表最清楚：

| 你执行的命令 | 实际作用 | 会不会把 skill 目录同步到 GitHub 最新代码 |
|---|---|---|
| git clone ... "$env:USERPROFILE\\.codex\\skills\\memorytree-workflow" | 想重新拉一份 | 不会，因为这一步直接失败了，目标目录已存在 |
| npm install | 安装依赖，并触发 build | 不会，只会编译“当前目录里现有的代码” |
| npm link | 把当前目录注册成全局 memorytree 命令 | 不会，只是让命令指向当前目录 |

所以关键点是：

你的 git clone 根本没成功。
这就意味着 [C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow) 并没有被 GitHub 最新提交覆盖。

但是为什么“GitHub 上传阻塞”又好了？

因为你当前这个 skill 目录不是“纯旧版本”，而是：

旧提交 b77a2c3 + 本地未提交修改

也就是说，它是一个“混合状态”：

- 基础版本还是旧的
- 但目录里已经带着一些本地改动
- 你执行 npm install 时，编译的是这份“混合代码”
- npm link 又把这份“混合代码”挂成了全局命令

所以就出现了现在这个现象：

| 现象 | 原因 |
|---|---|
| Push remote / Push URL 出现了 | 因为当前 skill 目录里这部分本地修改已经存在，并且被编译进去了 |
| .gitignore updated: ... 没出现 | 因为这部分新逻辑还没进入当前 skill 目录 |
| push 成功了 | 说明当前运行时里，与 push 相关的修复已经生效 |
| 但输出不是你预期的新版本输出 | 因为整个 skill 目录并没有完整同步到最新提交 |

一句人话总结：

你这次不是把 skill “升级到最新版本”，而是把“当前那份半旧半新的代码”重新编译后启用了。

所以：
- 有些问题好了
- 有些新输出还没进来

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接帮你把 [C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow) 真正同步到 0922e7e，把这个“混合状态”变成“完全最新状态”。

所以：

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接帮你把 [C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow) 真正同步到 0922e7e，把这个“混合状态”变成“完全最新状态”。

      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients, and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}
user #789 03:12:38

为什么出现这种混乱 ，没有拉取成功 为什么会 出现半新 半旧。 还有一个更重要的问题，如果更新，直接按全新拉取 为什么不会更新

本质上是两件事叠在一起了： 1. 你想更新的是“正在被 `memorytree` 命令执行的 skill 目录” 2. 但你执行的 `git clone` 没成功，所以那个目录根本没被 GitHub 最新代码覆盖 **为什么会出现“半新半旧”** 通俗讲，`C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow` 这个目录，本来就不是一份干净的旧版本，它是： - Git 提交还是旧的：`b77a...

Expand full message

本质上是两件事叠在一起了：

1. 你想更新的是“正在被 memorytree 命令执行的 skill 目录”
2. 但你执行的 git clone 没成功，所以那个目录根本没被 GitHub 最新代码覆盖

为什么会出现“半新半旧”

通俗讲，C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow 这个目录，本来就不是一份干净的旧版本，它是：

- Git 提交还是旧的：b77a2c3
- 但目录里已经有一些本地未提交修改

然后你又做了两步：

- npm install
- npm link

这两步的关键点是：

它们不会看 GitHub，也不会看 commit 号。
它们只会看“你当前磁盘上这个目录里到底是什么文件”。

所以就变成了：

- 某些文件已经被本地改过了，于是这些行为变“新”了
- 某些文件还没改，于是这些行为还是“旧” 的

这就是“半新半旧”。

你可以把它理解成：

- Git 提交 = 仓库里登记在册的版本
- 工作目录文件 = 你桌面上现在摊着的纸

npm build 编译的是“桌面上的纸”，不是“登记在册的版本”。

所以只要工作目录本身就是混合状态，编译出来的运行结果也会是混合状态。

为什么你那个上传阻塞问题好了，但 .gitignore 输出没出现

因为“好了的那部分”和“没同步的那部分”不是同一回事。

准确说：

- 这次 memorytree 实际运行的目录仍然是旧提交 b77a2c3
- 但这个旧目录里已经混进了一些本地修改
- 所以某些行为改变了
- 但不是全部都变成了 GitHub 最新版本

所以：

- “某个问题消失了” 不等于 “已经完整升级到最新提交”
- 它只说明 “当前运行目录里的某些本地文件，刚好已经变了”

更重要的问题：为什么“直接全新拉取”不会更新

因为 git clone 根本不是“更新命令”，它是“新建副本命令”。

它的规则是：

- 目标目录不存在，或者为空：可以 clone
- 目标目录已经存在且不是空目录：直接报错退出
- 报错后，Git 不会帮你覆盖，不会帮你合并，也不会偷偷更新一点点

你这次就属于第二种，所以它明确报了：

already exists and is not an empty directory

这句话的真正意思就是：

“我什么都没改。”

所以你后面的 npm install / npm link，其实都是在“原来那个旧目录”上继续操作。

一句话总结

- git clone 失败后，旧目录原封不动保留
- 旧目录里本来又有一些本地未提交改动
- npm install / npm link 又把这份“混合目录”编译并启用了
- 所以你看到的是“半新半旧”，不是“完整最新版”

真正的更新方式只有两类

1. 更新已有目录
先处理本地改动，再在这个目录里 git pull

2. 真正全新拉取
先把旧目录移走或删掉，再 clone 到一个空目录

所以“直接全新拉取为什么不会更新”的答案就是：

因为目标目录不是空的，git clone 从设计上就不会覆盖它。

如果你愿意，我下一条可以直接给你一份“最稳妥的 skill 更新流程”，分成：
- 保留本地修改版
- 完全重装版

total 13241118in 14601080out 167794reason 43744

- 所以某些行为改变了

- 目标目录不存在，或者为空：可以 clone
- 目标目录已经存在且不是空目录：直接报错退出
- 报错后，Git 不会帮你覆盖，不会帮你合并，也不会偷偷更新一点点

你这次就属于第二种，所以它明确报了：

already exists and is not an empty directory

这句话的真正意思就是：

“我什么都没改。”

所以你后面的 npm install / npm link，其实都是在“原来那个旧目录”上继续操作。

一句话总结

- git clone 失败后，旧目录原封不动保留
- 旧目录里本来又有一些本地未提交改动
- npm install / npm link 又把这份“混合目录”编译并启用了
- 所以你看到的是“半新半旧”，不是“完整最新版”

真正的更新方式只有两类

1. 更新已有目录
先处理本地改动，再在这个目录里 git pull

2. 真正全新拉取
先把旧目录移走或删掉，再 clone 到一个空目录

所以“直接全新拉取为什么不会更新”的答案就是：

因为目标目录不是空的，git clone 从设计上就不会覆盖它。

如果你愿意，我下一条可以直接给你一份“最稳妥的 skill 更新流程”，分成：
- 保留本地修改版
- 完全重装版

Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients, and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

Using Node v20.20.1
<div align="center">

<img src="assets/logo.svg" alt="MemoryTree" width="120" />

# MemoryTree Workflow

**Git-tracked project memory, transcript indexing, worktree-safe heartbeat automation, and report publishing for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI.**

<p>
  <code>Worktree-Backed Heartbeat</code> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
  <code>Cross-Client Recall</code> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
  <code>Managed Caddy Hosting</code> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
  <code>Static Report Publishing</code>
</p>

[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)
[![Node.js 20+](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node.js-20%2B-339933.svg?logo=node.js&logoColor=white)](https://nodejs.org/)
[![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/Platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-lightgrey.svg)](#requirements)

<p>
  <a href="#quick-start">Quick Start</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
  <a href="#architecture">Architecture</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
  <a href="#capabilities">Capabilities</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
  <a href="#heartbeat">Heartbeat</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
  <a href="#caddy">Caddy</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
  <a href="#cli">CLI</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
  <a href="#reference-docs">Docs</a>
</p>

</div>

---

## Overview

MemoryTree gives AI-assisted repositories a durable memory layer that survives across sessions, clients, branches, and machines.

This repository ships two things:

1. A reusable skill defined by [SKILL.md](SKILL.md)
2. A Node.js CLI for workspace setup, transcript import, session recall, report generation, heartbeat automation, and managed local hosting

The current implementation is built around three storage scopes:

- The development repository you actively edit
- A dedicated MemoryTree worktree that the heartbeat can update safely
- A global archive under `~/.memorytree/` for cross-project transcript storage and indexing

That model lets MemoryTree keep your main development flow clean while still importing transcripts, generating reports, and optionally publishing Memory-only updates from an isolated branch.

## Quick Start

### Install as a skill

Install the skill once on your machine, then use it from any repository.

You need Node.js 20 or newer.

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow "$env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow"
cd "$env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow"
npm install
npm run build
npm link
```

macOS:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link
```

Ubuntu:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link
```

If you use Claude Code, replace `.codex` with `.claude` in the path above. If your skill installer expects a local path instead of a git URL, point it at the repository root. `SKILL.md` lives at the top level.

After the skill is linked, open any target repository and run:

```bash
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

That `quick-start` command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.

End-to-end examples:

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
cd C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link

cd C:\path\to\your-project
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

macOS:

```bash
cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link

cd ~/path/to/your-project
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

Ubuntu:

```bash
cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link

cd ~/path/to/your-project
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

Notes:
- Do not run `quick-start` inside the skill repository unless you intentionally want to register the skill repository itself.
- `--root` must include a value such as `.` or an absolute path. `memorytree daemon quick-start --root` is incomplete and will fail.

If the shell says `memorytree` is not recognized, the skill is not on your PATH yet. Go back to the skill repository and run `npm link` again, then retry the command.

Quick check:

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
Get-Command memorytree
```

macOS / Ubuntu:

```bash
command -v memorytree
```

### Use the skill

In Claude Code, run:

```text
/memorytree-workflow
```

The skill detects whether the current repository is `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`, then takes the next appropriate step immediately.

### Use the CLI directly

`memorytree init` and `memorytree upgrade` only create or update repository files. They do not register the repository with heartbeat or modify `~/.memorytree/config.toml` by themselves.

Recommended first-time setup for a repository that should use heartbeat:

```bash
memorytree upgrade --root . --format json
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
memorytree caddy enable --root .
```

Quick Start keeps the dedicated `memorytree` branch as the shared source of truth and refreshes `AGENTS.md` plus `Memory/01_goals` through `Memory/05_archive` into your development directory as local cache mirrors, while `Memory/06_transcripts` and `Memory/07_reports` flow back from the memory worktree.
Its recommended defaults are `memorytree`, `heartbeat_interval = "5m"`, `auto_push = true`, `generate_report = true`, and `raw_upload_permission = "not-set"`, so raw transcript mirror commits stay disabled until you explicitly approve them.

Useful day-to-day commands:

```bash
memorytree recall --root .
memorytree discover --root . --client all --scope current-project --format json
memorytree report build --root . --no-ai --locale en
memorytree caddy status --root .
memorytree daemon run-once --root . --force
```

## Architecture

MemoryTree now runs as a worktree-aware single-source system, not just a single-folder transcript importer:

```text
Development repository (local cache mirror)
  AGENTS.md
  Memory/01_goals ... Memory/05_archive
        |
        | sync active context into
        v
Dedicated MemoryTree worktree (shared source of truth)
  isolated memorytree branch
  Memory/06_transcripts
  Memory/07_reports
        |
        | mirror matching transcripts, commit/push Memory-only changes,
        | build report output
        v
Global archive ~/.memorytree/
  config.toml
  alerts.json
  worktrees/
  transcripts/raw
  transcripts/clean
  transcripts/index
  caddy/
```

The dedicated MemoryTree branch is the only Git-backed shared memory source. The development directory is a local cache mirror for `AGENTS.md` plus `Memory/01_goals` through `Memory/05_archive`: heartbeat copies that active context into the dedicated worktree, processes transcript imports there, and then syncs refreshed `Memory/06_transcripts` and `Memory/07_reports` back into the directory you are actively using.

### Per-repository layout

After initialization or upgrade, a repository can contain:

```text
AGENTS.md
Memory/
  01_goals/
  02_todos/
  03_chat_logs/
  04_knowledge/
  05_archive/
  06_transcripts/
    clean/
    manifests/
    raw/
```

`Memory/07_reports/` is generated later by `memorytree report build`.

### Global layout

Shared state lives under `~/.memorytree/`:

```text
~/.memorytree/
  config.toml
  alerts.json
  heartbeat.lock
  logs/
  worktrees/
  caddy/
    Caddyfile
    sites/
  transcripts/
    raw/
    clean/
    index/
      manifests/
      sessions.jsonl
      search.sqlite
```

## Capabilities

| Capability | Current behavior |
|---|---|
| Workspace scaffolding | `init` and `upgrade` create `Memory/` plus `AGENTS.md` without overwriting stronger existing repo policy |
| Transcript archive | `discover` scans default client stores, while `import` handles explicit transcript files and mirrors matching sessions into the repo |
| Session recall | `recall` runs an on-demand sync and returns the latest prior session for the current repository across clients |
| Worktree-backed heartbeat | `daemon register` and `daemon quick-start` create an isolated MemoryTree worktree, schedule runs, and keep the shared memory branch plus development cache mirrors in sync |
| Report generation | `report build` creates a multi-page static site with transcripts, goals, todos, knowledge, archive, projects, graph, search, tags, and RSS |
| Managed local hosting | `caddy enable|disable|status` writes project-scoped Caddy fragments for long-running local report hosting |
| Git safety | Automatic repo-local commits stay confined to MemoryTree branches and skip raw transcript mirrors until raw uploads are approved |

## Transcript Sources

Default discovery currently scans these client stores:

| Client | Source patterns |
|---|---|
| Codex | `~/.codex/sessions/**/*.jsonl` |
| Claude Code | `~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl` |
| Gemini CLI | `~/.gemini/tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.json`, `~/.gemini/tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.jsonl`, `~/.gemini/history/**/*.json`, `~/.gemini/history/**/*.jsonl`, `~/.gemini/chats/**/*.json`, `~/.gemini/chats/**/*.jsonl` |

Explicit one-off import also supports transcript files that are not part of default discovery, including `doubao` exports:

```bash
memorytree import --source /path/to/doubao_export.txt --client doubao --root . --force-repo
```

Import rules:

- Raw transcripts stay unchanged as evidence.
- Clean transcripts are generated deterministically by code.
- Repo-local mirrors are limited to transcripts that belong to the current repository unless `--force-repo` is used deliberately.
- Unrelated transcripts are archived in the global archive only.
- Raw transcript mirrors are excluded from automatic staging until the user approves raw uploads for that repository.

## Session Continuity

When you ask for the most recent prior conversation, MemoryTree can:

1. scan the supported local transcript stores immediately
2. import any new matching transcripts
3. query the global index for the current project
4. exclude the current session by activation time
5. return the latest prior clean transcript content and metadata

This works across clients, so a project session recorded in one assistant can be recalled from another.

## Report Site

`memorytree report build` generates a static HTML site under `Memory/07_reports/`.

Current report coverage includes:

- dashboard
- transcript list and per-session pages
- goals, todos, knowledge, archive, and projects pages
- search, filters, and snippets
- graph view and backlink navigation
- tags and cached AI summaries
- breadcrumbs and table of contents
- RSS feed
- optional GitHub Pages deployment
- optional webhook notification
- English and Simplified Chinese locales

Build it manually:

```bash
memorytree report build --root . --no-ai --locale en --report-base-url https://memory.example.com
```

Temporary fallback preview:

```bash
memorytree report serve --dir ./Memory/07_reports --port 10010
```

`report serve` is intended for temporary preview. For long-running local access, keep Caddy pointed at `Memory/07_reports/`.

## Heartbeat

The heartbeat is the background automation layer. It is now project-aware and worktree-aware.

Recommended setup:

```bash
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

Use this as the quick-install path when you want the current repository to join heartbeat with the recommended defaults.

Quick Start keeps the dedicated `memorytree` branch as the shared source of truth and treats your development directory as a local cache mirror.
Its recommended defaults are `memorytree`, `heartbeat_interval = "5m"`, `auto_push = true`, `generate_report = true`, and `raw_upload_permission = "not-set"`.

If you want more control, install the machine scheduler once and then register each repository with your own branch, heartbeat cadence, and report settings:

```bash
memorytree daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true
memorytree daemon register --root . --branch memorytree-docs --heartbeat-interval 10m --auto-push false --generate-report true --report-port 10010
memorytree daemon run-once --root . --force
```

`memorytree daemon install` is the machine-level step, while `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` is the one-command repository setup. Both are cross-platform; only the initial skill installation differs by OS.

If `memorytree` is missing in a fresh shell, fix the skill installation first and then rerun `quick-start`.

The heartbeat now drives both transcript import and cache-mirror refresh timing. `refresh_interval` is no longer part of the runtime or config surface.

Current execution flow:

```text
Acquire lock
  -> load config
  -> select due projects
  -> ensure dedicated MemoryTree worktree
  -> sync AGENTS.md + Memory/01_goals through Memory/05_archive into the worktree
  -> discover and import matching transcripts
  -> build/update Memory/07_reports when report generation is enabled
  -> commit/push Memory/01-05 changes on the MemoryTree branch when allowed
  -> sync Memory/06_transcripts and Memory/07_reports back to the development directory
  -> release lock
```

Important branch-safety rules:

- Repo-local transcript mirroring and automatic commits only happen on a dedicated MemoryTree branch.
- On non-MemoryTree branches, the heartbeat still imports into the global archive but keeps the repository clean.
- If no Git remote exists, push is skipped and an alert is written.
- Push failures alert and retry once.
- Sensitive transcript patterns are logged as warnings only; they are not auto-deleted or auto-redacted.

## Caddy

MemoryTree includes first-stage project-managed Caddy support for local report hosting.

Recommended commands:

```bash
memorytree caddy enable --root .
memorytree caddy status --root .
memorytree caddy disable --root .
```

What it manages:

- `~/.memorytree/caddy/Caddyfile`
- `~/.memorytree/caddy/sites/<project-id>.caddy`
- per-project port and exposure settings taken from `~/.memorytree/config.toml`

Exposure modes:

- `local`: bind to `127.0.0.1` and `localhost` only
- `lan`: bind on the configured port and expose LAN URLs for the machine's IPv4 interfaces

For long-running local access, `caddy enable` is the primary path. `report serve` remains the lightweight fallback.

## Configuration

Global settings live in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`.

Example:

```toml
heartbeat_interval = "5m"
auto_push = true
log_level = "info"
generate_report = true
locale = "en"
gh_pages_branch = "gh-pages"
cname = "memory.example.com"
webhook_url = ""
report_base_url = "https://memory.example.com"
report_port = 10010
report_exposure = "local"

[[projects]]
id = "my-repo"
path = "/path/to/repo"
name = "my-repo"
development_path = "/path/to/repo"
memory_path = "/path/to/.memorytree/worktrees/my-repo"
memory_branch = "memorytree"
heartbeat_interval = "5m"
auto_push = true
generate_report = true
raw_upload_permission = "not-set"
locale = "en"
gh_pages_branch = "gh-pages"
report_base_url = "https://memory.example.com"
report_port = 10010
report_exposure = "local"
```

Fields that matter most:

| Field | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `development_path` | The directory you actively work in |
| `memory_path` | The dedicated MemoryTree worktree used by the heartbeat |
| `memory_branch` | The branch reserved for MemoryTree automation |
| `heartbeat_interval` | Per-project heartbeat cadence |
| `generate_report` | Whether heartbeat should build `Memory/07_reports` after transcript imports |
| `raw_upload_permission` | Whether repo-local raw transcript mirrors may be auto-committed: `not-set`, `approved`, or `denied` |
| `gh_pages_branch` | Branch used for report publishing when configured |
| `report_base_url` | Base URL for RSS and OG metadata |
| `report_port` | Default local report port for this project |
| `report_exposure` | `local` or `lan` exposure for managed Caddy hosting |

`path` is still preserved for compatibility, but the worktree-aware flow is driven by `development_path` and `memory_path`.

## Git Safety

MemoryTree is strict about not taking over your normal development flow.

| Rule | Behavior |
|---|---|
| Branch isolation | Automatic repo-local transcript commits happen only on dedicated MemoryTree branches |
| Worktree isolation | Heartbeat automation can run from a dedicated worktree instead of your main working directory |
| Scope isolation | Repo mirrors include only the current project's transcripts unless you force a repo-local import |
| Raw upload control | Raw transcript files are not auto-staged until explicitly approved |
| Push safety | If no remote exists, push is skipped and an alert is written |
| Failure handling | Push failures alert and retry once; report and webhook failures do not abort the heartbeat cycle |
| Secret scanning | Transcript imports log potential secrets but do not auto-delete or auto-redact them |

## CLI

All commands are available through `node dist/cli.js <subcommand> ...`, or `memorytree <subcommand> ...` if you ran `npm link`.

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `memorytree init` | Initialize MemoryTree files in a repository without registering heartbeat |
| `memorytree upgrade` | Upgrade repository files to MemoryTree without registering heartbeat |
| `memorytree import --source <file>` | Import one transcript into the repo mirror and global archive |
| `memorytree discover` | Scan local client stores and import matching transcripts |
| `memorytree locale` | Detect the effective repository locale |
| `memorytree recall` | Run on-demand sync and return the latest prior session |
| `memorytree report build` | Build the static HTML report site |
| `memorytree report serve` | Temporarily preview the generated report over HTTP |
| `memorytree caddy enable` | Write/update the current project's managed Caddy fragment and reload Caddy |
| `memorytree caddy disable` | Remove the current project's managed Caddy fragment and reload Caddy |
| `memorytree caddy status` | Show whether the current project is connected to MemoryTree-managed Caddy |
| `memorytree daemon install` | Register the machine-level heartbeat scheduler |
| `memorytree daemon quick-start` | Quick install: connect the repository to heartbeat with the shared memory branch + local cache mirror defaults |
| `memorytree daemon register` | Advanced heartbeat setup with custom per-project settings |
| `memorytree daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task |
| `memorytree daemon run-once` | Execute one heartbeat cycle immediately |
| `memorytree daemon watch` | Development-only continuous heartbeat loop |
| `memorytree daemon status` | Show scheduler registration and lock status |

## Development And Validation

Install dependencies:

```bash
npm install
```

Build:

```bash
npm run build
```

Validate locally:

```bash
npm run typecheck
npm run lint
npm test
npm run test:e2e
```

Current validation shape:

- Node.js 20+ is required locally
- CI runs on Node.js 20, 22, and 24 across Linux, macOS, and Windows
- The repository includes focused unit coverage for transcript parsing/import, project upgrade, report rendering, heartbeat orchestration, and Caddy management
- The E2E workflow runs the CLI end-to-end on Node.js 20 across Linux, macOS, and Windows
- Both GitHub Actions workflows also support manual `workflow_dispatch` reruns when you want an on-demand cross-platform check before or after a merge

Current cross-platform E2E coverage includes:

- transcript import, discover, recall, and report build/serve
- dedicated worktree registration plus immediate `daemon run-once` execution
- de-tracked development directories staying clean while cache mirrors refresh from the worktree
- snapshot commits when no new transcripts are imported but synced context changes
- raw transcript mirror policy checks for approved vs. unapproved uploads
- ignored `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/**` paths still being committed correctly on the dedicated MemoryTree branch
- branch-safety behavior on non-MemoryTree branches
- report deployment and webhook failure tolerance

| [references/upgrade-path.md](references/upgrade-path.md) | Non-destructive adoption path |

## Update

Claude Code:

```bash
cd ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow
git pull
npm install
npm run build
```

Codex:

```bash
cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
git pull
npm install
npm run build
```

## Requirements

- Node.js 20 or newer
- npm
- Git
- Claude Code, Codex, or another environment that can consume the skill or CLI

MIT

      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients, and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

Using Node v20.20.1
---
name: memorytree-workflow
description: Detect, initialize, and maintain a reusable MemoryTree project-memory workspace for any code repository. On activation, immediately detect and report the repo's MemoryTree state.
---

# MemoryTree

Use this skill for repo-level project memory. Do not treat it as an application runtime feature unless the user explicitly asks to productize it.

## On Activation

When this skill is invoked, immediately perform these steps without waiting for further user input:

1. Detect the repo state (`not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`) using `references/project-detection.md`.
2. Check `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. If pending alerts exist, display them to the user before proceeding.
3. If `not-installed`: run `memorytree init` to scaffold the workspace, then report what was created.
4. If `partial`: run `memorytree upgrade` to add missing pieces, then report what was added.
5. If `installed`: read the active goal, bound todo, and latest chat log. Report the current state (goal summary, todo progress, pending tasks) and ask the user what they want to work on next. If `memorytree daemon status` shows the heartbeat is not registered, ask the user whether they want the recommended current-repository setup via `memorytree daemon quick-start --root <repo>` or only a machine-level scheduler restore via `memorytree daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true`. Use `memorytree daemon register` only when they explicitly want custom branch, interval, report-port, or worktree settings. Use plain-text questions compatible with all AI assistants, not client-specific UI components.

Do not stop after detection to ask whether you should proceed. Act on the result immediately.

## Core Behavior

1. Detect the repo state before changing files. Use `references/project-detection.md`.
2. If MemoryTree is missing, initialize it with `memorytree init`. If the CLI is not available, create the files from `assets/templates/`.
3. If MemoryTree is partial, upgrade it with `memorytree upgrade` before manual maintenance.
4. If MemoryTree is already installed, maintain only the active goal, bound todo, and current chat log. Use `references/update-rules.md`.
5. Keep the installation compatible with the host repo's branch, PR, CI, and review rules. Use `references/git-policy.md`.
6. Prefer safe defaults: do not add reply suffix requirements unless the host repository already requires them, do not mix code-and-memory commits by default, and do not override branch policy. MemoryTree-only git automation is allowed only inside the isolated flow in `references/git-policy.md`.
7. When locale is not explicit, choose it from repo language first, then system language. Use `references/locale-selection.md`.
8. Keep reply language aligned with user language first, then repo locale. Use `references/response-language.md`.
9. For partial repos, use the non-destructive upgrade path before manual maintenance. Use `references/upgrade-path.md`.
10. When transcript archival is enabled, import transcripts with deterministic code, preserve raw transcripts as evidence in both the current repo mirror and the global archive, generate cleaned Markdown for indexing, keep cross-project backfills and ambiguous ownership cases in the global archive only, and ask once per repository whether raw transcript files may be committed or pushed. Use `references/transcript-archive.md`.
11. When CLI commands are needed, use `memorytree <subcommand>` (requires Node.js 鈮?20). If Node.js is not available, fall back to manual scaffolding from `assets/templates/`. See `references/execution-environment.md`.
12. Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
13. Global configuration is stored in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`. See `references/global-configuration.md`.
14. When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand transcript sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all three clients, and generate a continuation summary. See `references/transcript-archive.md` and `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
15. All interactive prompts (heartbeat install, configuration, session continuity) must use plain-text questions compatible with every AI assistant that supports this skill. Do not rely on client-specific UI components. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

## Read Order

1. Repo `AGENTS.md`, if present.
2. Latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
3. Latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
4. `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
5. `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/`, transcript manifests, and repo-local raw transcript mirrors only when transcript history or cross-project memory search is relevant.
6. `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Detect

1. Check whether `Memory/` exists and matches the layout in `references/memory-layout.md`.
2. Classify the repo as `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`.
3. If the repo already has stronger rules than the generated templates, preserve the repo rules and only add missing MemoryTree pieces.

## Upgrade

1. Use `memorytree upgrade` for repositories that already have `AGENTS.md`, custom policy files, or partial `Memory/` content.
2. Preserve existing repo policy files by default.
3. Create only the missing MemoryTree pieces.
4. Read the upgrade result and treat `agents_merge_required=true` as a manual review task, not an overwrite instruction.

## Initialize

1. Run `memorytree init --root <repo>` and pass `--project-name`, `--goal-summary`, and `--locale` when the user already provided them.
2. Use fresh init only when the repo does not already have `AGENTS.md` or another repo policy source such as a policy-bearing `CONTRIBUTING.md`, PR templates, `CODEOWNERS`, or commitlint config. If repo policy already exists, switch to `memorytree upgrade` instead of trying to skip `AGENTS.md`.
3. Review the generated `AGENTS.md` before keeping it. Merge it with the repo's existing policy if needed.
4. Keep the initial installation minimal: `Memory/01_goals`, `02_todos`, `03_chat_logs`, optional `04_knowledge`, `05_archive`, and `06_transcripts` only when transcript archival is enabled for the repo or needed for transcript import.
5. Use the locale-specific template files in `assets/templates/<locale>/` as the source of truth for first-run scaffolding.
6. Prefer `--locale auto` when the user did not request a language explicitly.
7. Use `memorytree locale` when you need a deterministic locale check without scaffolding files.
8. If Node.js is not available, create first-run files directly from `assets/templates/` instead of requiring a runtime installation.
9. If transcript archival is enabled for the repo, use `memorytree import --source <file>` for one explicit source file and `memorytree discover` when scanning local client stores. Matching transcripts must update both the repo mirror and the global archive, while unrelated projects, ambiguous ownership cases, or mismatched explicit sources are archived in the global archive only. Let the CLI generate cleaned transcripts deterministically without spending model tokens on the cleaning step.
10. Ask once whether raw transcript files may also be committed to the repository. Record the answer in `AGENTS.md`, keep the files in the repo either way, and exclude `raw` files from automatic staging until the user says yes.

## Maintain

1. Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change.
2. Update the active todo when progress changes, milestones move, or the next task becomes clearer.
3. Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
4. Keep active files small. Move stale context to archive or knowledge files instead of bloating the active goal or todo.
5. Treat product ideas, architecture decisions, and operating constraints as goal or knowledge content. Treat step-by-step execution state as todo content.
6. Treat cleaned transcript indexes as discovery aids, not as the final source of truth. When exact wording matters, confirm against the raw transcript archive.
7. Prefer deterministic CLI-based transcript cleaning over manual rewriting. Do not spend model tokens to restate or normalize transcript content when `memorytree import` can do it.
8. Reply in the user's language when clear; otherwise align with repo locale without rewriting existing files just to translate them.

## Git And Safety

1. Never assume MemoryTree can override the repo's branch model.
2. Default to isolated MemoryTree-only Git operations: stage only MemoryTree-owned files, use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title, push to a dedicated branch, and open a dedicated PR.
3. Enable auto-merge only for MemoryTree-only PRs, and only when the repository still enforces its required review and CI checks.
4. Keep repo-local raw transcript mirrors and the global transcript archive synchronized on every import. The global index must remain safe for concurrent multi-project updates.
5. Keep the repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current repository. Unrelated or ambiguous transcripts belong in the global archive only.
6. Keep raw transcript files under `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` in the repo, but exclude them from automatic staging until the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for that repository.
7. If the diff mixes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript archives, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before committing or pushing.
8. If the repo has protected branches or PR-only rules, follow them even if older MemoryTree rules say otherwise.
9. When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no Git remote is configured, the push is skipped. If a push fails, the heartbeat retries once. In either case an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. See `references/git-policy.md`.
10. During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for sensitive information (API keys, passwords, tokens). Matches are logged as warnings only 鈥?no automatic deletion or redaction. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

11. Do not modify the host repository's CI or E2E workflow definitions by default. If the user explicitly approves a MemoryTree-only optimization, prefer path-filtered or lightweight workflows for `Memory/**` and managed `AGENTS.md` PRs without weakening required branch protection.

## Resources

- `references/project-detection.md`: detect install state and choose init vs maintain.
- `references/memory-layout.md`: folder and file naming rules.
- `references/update-rules.md`: when to version goals, todos, and session logs.
- `references/git-policy.md`: safe Git defaults and policy merge guidance.
- `references/locale-selection.md`: locale choice rules and alias behavior.
- `references/response-language.md`: reply-language precedence and translation safety.
- `references/transcript-archive.md`: raw transcript archival, clean transcript indexing, per-client source rules, and repo upload confirmation rules.
- `references/upgrade-path.md`: safe adoption flow for partial repos.
- `references/execution-environment.md`: how to run MemoryTree CLI (requires Node.js 鈮?20).
- `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`: background heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, and execution flow.
- `references/global-configuration.md`: `~/.memorytree/` directory layout, `config.toml` schema, and `alerts.json` format.
- `assets/templates/en/` and `assets/templates/zh-cn/`: seed files for English and Simplified Chinese initialization.
- CLI commands: `memorytree init`, `memorytree upgrade`, `memorytree import`, `memorytree discover`, `memorytree locale`, `memorytree recall`, `memorytree daemon install|uninstall|run-once|watch|status|quick-start|register`.

# Project Goal v001

## Metadata
- Version: v001
- Created: 2026-03-24
- Previous: none

## North Star
Keep the MemoryTree Workflow repository on the main branch, synchronized with origin, and maintain a minimal working project-memory structure.

## Scope
- Define the stable objective.
- Define constraints and non-goals.

## Acceptance Signals
- The repository is on `main` and up to date with `origin/main`.
- The MemoryTree active files exist and are readable.
- Future MemoryTree updates can append to the current goal, todo, and chat log without reinitialization.

273:| Worktree-backed heartbeat | `daemon register` and `daemon quick-start` create an isolated MemoryTree worktree, schedule runs, and keep the shared memory branch plus development cache mirrors in sync |

369:`memorytree daemon install` is the machine-level step, while `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` is the one-command repository setup. Both are cross-platform; only the initial skill installation differs by OS.
371:If `memorytree` is missing in a fresh shell, fix the skill installation first and then rerun `quick-start`.
